Title: Head of Communications AUNZ (AU)
Description: With the best voice in the band, the Communications team speaks to an audience of millions every day.We’re storytellers, tasked with bringing what Spotify stands for to life on the global stage. Wherever and whenever our voice is heard, we ensure it remains authentic to the brand and supports business growth.Join us and help to protect and promote Spotify’s unique sound for years to come.This role will lead all communications, internal and external, for Spotify across Australia and New Zealand, providing strategic direction and execution across consumer, corporate and trade communications. This role is diverse and demanding and requires a creative, motivated, independent, self-starting critical thinker, who is prepared to work independently across a wide variety of tasks at all levels.As the senior communications lead in these markets, this individual should be adept at working across stakeholder groups and making effective comms recommendations in line with overall business objectives, goals and audiences for Spotify in AUNZ. It is critical that you are able to seamlessly coordinate with the business head of the region, as well as the broader global comms team, to guide on what is best suited to your markets, whilst also providing counsel and high-level comms support to the senior executives in your region.In this role, you are expected to be comfortable with fast-paced, complex scenarios whilst operating at all levels of the comms spectrum. You possess strong business acumen, writing and project management skills, and are able to drive the agenda when engaging with stakeholders across the company and at the highest level across the business.You are expected to thoughtfully consider Spotify’s business and reputational goals, and execute them in strategic, proactive and globally integrated ways. The role may also be at the front line of a diverse range of time-sensitive issues and crisis management situations, requiring a cool head, problem solving skills and impeccable communications skills.What You’ll DoAUNZ Comms Strategy:Lead the development, refining, and execution of a communications strategy for your markets, with Australia being the primary focusMedia Relations:Build and maintain strong relations with key media across AUNZ in order to drive communication objectives and generate accurate and positive coverageConsumer Communications:Set local strategy and implement communications campaigns across all channels, supporting influencer and partner engagement, along with supporting Spotify’s owned content strategyCorporate Communications:Lead all communications activities across AUNZ on behalf of Spotify, support senior executives and their activities, telling proactive stories that are aligned with Spotify’s reputation prioritiesCrisis Communications:Manage and oversee short-term reactive communications for your markets, including making smart assessments of the situation, creating a plan and acting quickly to get stakeholder buy-in, including developing reactive statements and key messagesMusic & Podcast Industry Communications: Communicate Spotify’s value to the creator audience and our work to connect artists/creators and fans by building relationships and understanding market challenges. Position Spotify as the #1 destination for Music and Podcasts and the leader in audio.Trade Communications: Build Spotify’s profile as a leading advertising platform to brand and marketing trade audiencesStakeholder Engagement: Engage and drive buy-in from senior level stakeholders at the highest level across the business, including the AUNZ management team and regional stakeholdersInternal Communications: Set local strategy and lead internal communications activities in AUNZAgency Management: Manage PR agency support in the AUNZ: Provide the brief, direction and ongoing guidance to the agencyWho You AreMinimum of 10 to 12 years relevant communications with direct experience working in Consumer PR, digital, media, entertainment and music industry PR, or in the tech industry in AustraliaStrategic thinker with the ability to work in a fast paced, global environment; public company preferableProven ability to develop and execute strategic PR campaigns; solid experience working cross functionally to produce strong results a plusSolid media contacts and relationships in entertainment, music and tech communityWell-versed in shaping the narrative, creative storytelling across a variety of communications channels and audiencesMotivated self-starter with a hands-on attitudeTeam player who thinks and acts globally, even when directly dealing with a local challengeAbility to multitask and work in a hectic and extremely fast-moving industry and environment without sacrificing attention to detailAbility to work across all levels, no task is too big or too smallStrong project management and agency management skillsExcellent organisational skillsAbility to travel domestically and internationallyOf course, an ever-enthusiastic representative of the Spotify brandYou are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be brilliant. So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the opportunity to enjoy and be inspired by these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service with a community of more than 271 million users.

What You\u2019ll Do: AUNZ Comms Strategy:Lead the development, refining, and execution of a communications strategy for your markets, with Australia being the primary focus
Media Relations:Build and maintain strong relations with key media across AUNZ in order to drive communication objectives and generate accurate and positive coverage

Consumer Communications:Set local strategy and implement communications campaigns across all channels, supporting influencer and partner engagement, along with supporting Spotify’s owned content strategy

Corporate Communications:Lead all communications activities across AUNZ on behalf of Spotify, support senior executives and their activities, telling proactive stories that are aligned with Spotify’s reputation priorities

Crisis Communications:Manage and oversee short-term reactive communications for your markets, including making smart assessments of the situation, creating a plan and acting quickly to get stakeholder buy-in, including developing reactive statements and key messages

Music & Podcast Industry Communications: Communicate Spotify’s value to the creator audience and our work to connect artists/creators and fans by building relationships and understanding market challenges. Position Spotify as the #1 destination for Music and Podcasts and the leader in audio.
Trade Communications: Build Spotify’s profile as a leading advertising platform to brand and marketing trade audiences
Stakeholder Engagement: Engage and drive buy-in from senior level stakeholders at the highest level across the business, including the AUNZ management team and regional stakeholders
Internal Communications: Set local strategy and lead internal communications activities in AUNZ
Agency Management: Manage PR agency support in the AUNZ: Provide the brief, direction and ongoing guidance to the agency
Who You Are: Minimum of 10 to 12 years relevant communications with direct experience working in Consumer PR, digital, media, entertainment and music industry PR, or in the tech industry in Australia
Strategic thinker with the ability to work in a fast paced, global environment; public company preferable
Proven ability to develop and execute strategic PR campaigns; solid experience working cross functionally to produce strong results a plus
Solid media contacts and relationships in entertainment, music and tech community
Well-versed in shaping the narrative, creative storytelling across a variety of communications channels and audiences
Motivated self-starter with a hands-on attitude
Team player who thinks and acts globally, even when directly dealing with a local challenge
Ability to multitask and work in a hectic and extremely fast-moving industry and environment without sacrificing attention to detail
Ability to work across all levels, no task is too big or too small
Strong project management and agency management skills
Excellent organisational skills
Ability to travel domestically and internationally
Of course, an ever-enthusiastic representative of the Spotify brand

Title: Producer, New Show Development, Gimlet (US)
Description: Gimlet Media tells stories through audio. Our roster of podcasts includes critically-acclaimed shows like Reply All, Crimetown, Homecoming, and The Nod. We have top editorial, sales, and business teams that work together to create experiences that help listeners better understand each other and the world around them. We are looking for ambitious, curious, talented, and kind people to join our team.We come from diverse backgrounds, brought together by the desire to create amazing experiences for listeners, and a shared belief in working hard, embracing creativity, and supporting one another. At Gimlet, good ideas matter more than titles. People who tackle problems with creative solutions, and who love the energy of a collaborative, team-oriented creative process thrive here. We’re proud to have recently joined the Spotify family with whom we share these values, and we’re thrilled to begin building production capacity on the West Coast.The JobWe’re looking for an experienced producer to join our new show development team, based in Los Angeles.This is an omnivorous, wide-ranging role that will be tasked to help create new concepts, new formats and bring new voices to audio. The successful candidate will be a capable producer with experience shaping new shows and switching between roles. A new show development producer must be comfortable working on a wide variety of shows – from daily production to narrative documentary – and must switch between show formats with agility and enthusiasm. You’ll be involved in writing, editing and mixing and be trusted to listen for qualities that will captivate listeners.What You’ll DoBrainstorm, develop and shape concepts for new shows Collaborate, train and support Hosts and production staff in the development processCut tape, assemble mixes and score episodesPlan field producing and reporting trips in collaboration with supervising producersFind, pre-interview, and book guests Arrange for studio recordings and tape syncsWrite scriptsHelp grow and maintain the show’s social media presenceHelp out with basic show tasks, including posting the episodes to our CMSWhat You’ll BringA minimum of four years of experience producing audio for radio or podcastA journalism background An ability to listen to raw tape, and identify the perfect details, the need-to-know plot points, and the need-to-hear moments that make a storyExperience scripting 15-20 minute audio storiesProficiency in field recordingProficiency in creating assemblies in ProToolsAn eye for detail and self-directionYou are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be brilliant. So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the opportunity to enjoy and be inspired by these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service with a community of more than 271 million users.

The Job: We’re looking for an experienced producer to join our new show development team, based in Los Angeles.
What You\u2019ll Do: Brainstorm, develop and shape concepts for new shows
Collaborate, train and support Hosts and production staff in the development process
Cut tape, assemble mixes and score episodes
Plan field producing and reporting trips in collaboration with supervising producers
Find, pre-interview, and book guests
Arrange for studio recordings and tape syncs
Write scripts
Help grow and maintain the show’s social media presence
Help out with basic show tasks, including posting the episodes to our CMS
What You\u2019ll Bring: A minimum of four years of experience producing audio for radio or podcast
A journalism background
An ability to listen to raw tape, and identify the perfect details, the need-to-know plot points, and the need-to-hear moments that make a story
Experience scripting 15-20 minute audio stories
Proficiency in field recording
Proficiency in creating assemblies in ProTools
An eye for detail and self-direction

Title: Program Manager (US)
Description: The Apple Media Products (AMP) organization is an exciting environment with many opportunities for learning and development.With customers in 155 countries, we are a thriving marketplace for music, movies, TV shows, apps and books. We’re looking for an Engineering Program Manager (EPM) to drive initiatives related to Apple Music & partnerships with new clients.EPM’s at AMP are responsible for shipping new features. We’re looking for people with proven track records of shipping complex, customer-facing products under demanding timelines, with bonus points for new product introductions.This is a hands-on position where the EPM will be involved in requirements analysis, functional and technical design discussions, engineering implementation, QA testing, proof-of-concept, working with partners, and more. This is not a task-based job; you will be responsible for high-level successful outcomes and delivery. Ultimately, you are responsible for shipping the code which millions of customers will use.AMP EPM’s take responsibility, feeling a personal stake in the product they ship. They must communicate responsibilities and scope clearly, manage conflict, and thrive in uncertainty while striving to find solutions. They must build strong relationships across the entire organization, especially with Senior Directors & VP’s, as they’re frequently responsible for executive presentations. Autonomy is critical; EPM’s only receive consultative direction from leadership and must “figure it out” themselves. Multi-tasking and concise documentation are skills you’ll use every day.Key Qualifications5+ years of technical program management in software development.Self-starting, self-motivated, self-directed, self-responsible, self-sufficient, and accountable.Outstanding communication and presentation skills (written and verbal) to all levels of an organization.Strong facilitation skills (requirements sessions, design meetings, progress and status meetings).Strong technical proficiency. Bonus points for past programming experience.Business-savvy; MBA a plus.Passion for working on Music related products.DescriptionDrive and ship all aspects of large, cross-functional projects related to Apple Music & other partner projects.Oversee all aspects of multiple concurrent features including scope, requirements, timelines, and resource allocation.Define business, functional, and reporting requirements (often writing them yourself).Take a role in improving how features are built and executed on the AMP team.Own cross-functional schedules, including AMP development, other Apple groups (client teams, device teams, Apple business teams), and backend reporting systems.Collaborate with IS&T, Legal, Finance, Design, Ops, and other organizations both inside and outside of Apple to develop solutions for partnerships, promotions and new business.Experience with transactional systems, pricing and international currencies is a plus but not required.Education & ExperienceBA/BS with equivalent experience. Masters/MBA pr

Key Qualifications: 5+ years of technical program management in software development.
Self-starting, self-motivated, self-directed, self-responsible, self-sufficient, and accountable.

Strong technical proficiency. Bonus points for past programming experience.

This individual will assist the VP of Talent and liaise between departments in day-to-day tasks, concert booking procedures, venue calendar bookings and managing relationships in the live event industry. Title: Publicist (UK)

Artist & Label Services is the umbrella for WEA (Warner-Elektra-Atlantic) – the pioneering WEA distribution and marketing network – and Alternative Distribution Alliance (ADA) – the ground-breaking global distribution company for independent artists and labels.A little bit about our team:Atlantic Records UK has a world class roster of artists including Ed Sheeran, Jess Glynne, Clean Bandit, Plan B, Rudimental, James Blunt, Rita Ora, Charli XCX, Paolo Nutini, Anne-Marie, Mahalia, WSTRN and many more as well as an exciting joint venture with Stormzy and his #merky label. Their musical tastes, creative minds and eclecticism are embraced and a Senior Publicist will form a big part of the team’s development within the company and that of the artist’s here at Atlantic UK.Why this could be your next big break:Atlantic is one of the biggest front line labels in the UK and a senior member of the Publicity team will mainline into the UK’s most influential print and online media on behalf of our global talent, managing, curating and strategising around campaigns and profiles. We strive to set WMG apart by embracing a philosophy of innovation – it’s part of our company’s DNA.Consider a career at WMG and be a part of one of the most influential forces in culture today.Love this job and want to apply?Click the “Apply” link at the top of the page, or apply directly with your LinkedIn. We are based in the heart of the German capital’s creative hub employing more than 400 people across our seven offices in Berlin, Los Angeles, Tokyo, London, Toronto, Paris, and Shenzhen.We’re looking for a creative marketing professional (f/m/x) with a passion for music – Someone who understands music production and marketing, and knows how to combine the two.Working within the Release Marketing Team, you’ll develop and implement marketing campaigns and initiatives to promote our latest software and hardware releases for music production and DJs. The work is varied – requiring a diverse creative skillset across strategy, content, product marketing, digital marketing, video production, social media and more.What You’ll DoConceptualize, manage, and report on marketing initiatives and campaignsLead the production of marketing content with a focus on video, digital, and socialWork closely with Product Owners on product definition and communication strategyCollaborate with other teams in Marketing such as Artist Relations, Social Media, PR, Design, and Digital Marketing to successfully bring campaigns to lifeManage external stakeholders, partners and artistsDefine performance criteria and analyze the success of your campaignsActively follow and evaluate market trends and developmentsSupport the brand strategy by identifying new marketing opportunitiesIdentify areas of improvement as well as opportunities to use modern Marketing practicesWhat You’ll BringAt least 8 years’ experience in a marketing or creative environment, ideally in the MI industry or an agencyExperience in music production and/or sound designAn interest and understanding of the current music and music production landscapeHigh level of organization and excellent time management skillsCoaching and mentoring skills as well as proven experience in leading teams Strong systemic thinking; data-driven decision making; ability to find root causes of issues and gain stakeholder support to address opportunitiesAbility to be both hands on and strategic; willing to take on duties while also being able to delegate to and provide direction and motivation to a small teamProven experience owning projects and driving campaigns involving media productionExcellent English is a must, German is a plusCooperative, dynamic, and positive attitudeNative Instruments embraces diversity. As an equal opportunities employer, we believe the foundation of our dynamic and pioneering spirit starts with a fair and inclusive culture. Our company culture focuses on bringing unique ideas together to fuel innovation.All applicants will receive equal consideration for employment at Native Instruments and we encourage everyone to apply – regardless of gender identity, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or any other characteristic protected by law. Help us reach our goal in making the future of music diverse, inclusive, and exciting.

What You\u2019ll Do: Conceptualize, manage, and report on marketing initiatives and campaigns
Lead the production of marketing content with a focus on video, digital, and social
Work closely with Product Owners on product definition and communication strategy

Title: Label Manager (UK)
Description: Label Manager OverviewThe Orchard has an immediate opening for an experienced Label Manager in our London office. Managing key relationships you will be the first point of contact for a bespoke roster of distributed labels and artists across a variety of genres. You will act as a constant and reliable source of advice and expertise for your clients, always striving to add maximum value to their numerous releases and campaigns. You must have a minimum of two years relevant experience at a distributor, label or management company.A passion for music and the industry are a must. Your professional and confident approach will enable you to build and maintain incredibly strong relationships with labels and artist management, as well as key external partners, agencies and suppliers. The Orchard is a fast-paced environment, you’ll have plenty of support but we’ll also need you to stand on your own two feet, to work independently, conscientiously and with an entrepreneurial spirit.The Orchard is a leading music distribution company operating in over 40 markets worldwide – distributing music from independent artists including Skepta, Jorja Smith, Albert Hammond Jr. and Ezra Collective. With cutting edge operations and an unparalleled global team, The Orchard partners with labels of all sizes to make their music and video available across hundreds of digital and physical retailers around the world. At The Orchard, our focus is to provide a comfortable, social and engaging environment to encourage productivity and creativity.What you will do:Be the first point of contact and maintain day-to-day relationships for a varied portfolio of clientsLiaise with internal departments, from operations, product, sales and marketing to finance, legal and management to ensure all clients’ business needs are satisfied.Oversee scheduling of releases, ensuring deadlines are met and all represented sales and marketing needs are managed across digital and physical in the UK and abroad.Manage physical product logistics, including co-ordination of manufacturing when required and ensuring all stock requirements and deadlines are met.Proof digital metadata using Orchard technology systems to ensure all releases are delivered to required specifications and in a timely manner.Strategise and manage priority release campaign logistics from inception through to analysis, working with other internal departments, including retail marketing, digital marketing and advertising and any external partners including press, radio and marketing agencies.Supply Sales departments with all materials necessary for selling releases into retail with maximum impact.Consistently inform and educate labels with current and developing best practice across all business areas. Develop knowledge and relationships across the industry.Drive product adoption of The Orchard’s tools and services across client base.Represent The Orchard at events and conferences as required. Your Skills and Experience:Minimum 2 years relevant experience in a music distributor, label or management environmentA strong understanding of digital distribution, metadata requirements and digital marketing.Highly experienced in the creation and distribution life cycle of physical releases and associated deadlines from production through release date.Established relationships with a variety of publicists, press and radio companies.Highly advanced organisational skills with the ability to multitask and prioritise to tight deadlines.Excellent communication skills, with the ability to speak effectively across all levels of business and the capacity to identify key relationships and develop them accordingly.A willingness to approach new areas of business, with a positive and considered attitude.Awareness of changes and developments in the industry, with particular emphasis on competitor activity.Strong industry network and relationships within the independent label space.Fluent in English, additional languages desirable but not essential.Able to travel as required.The ideal candidate is:Patient and empathetic with all clients from label owners and artist managers to interns.Friendly, approachable and enthusiastic with an innate desire to understand the needs of clients.A brilliant all round communicator – on phone, email and in person.Someone with supreme attention to detail and follow-through skills.Resourceful and takes the initiative – an expert troubleshooter.Passionate about music and the music industry with drive and enthusiasm to excel.

What you will do:: Be the first point of contact and maintain day-to-day relationships for a varied portfolio of clients
Liaise with internal departments, from operations, product, sales and marketing to finance, legal and management to ensure all clients’ business needs are satisfied.
Oversee scheduling of releases, ensuring deadlines are met and all represented sales and marketing needs are managed across digital and physical in the UK and abroad.
Manage physical product logistics, including co-ordination of manufacturing when required and ensuring all stock requirements and deadlines are met.
Proof digital metadata using Orchard technology systems to ensure all releases are delivered to required specifications and in a timely manner.

Strategise and manage priority release campaign logistics from inception through to analysis, working with other internal departments, including retail marketing, digital marketing and advertising and any external partners including press, radio and marketing agencies.
Supply Sales departments with all materials necessary for selling releases into retail with maximum impact.
Consistently inform and educate labels with current and developing best practice across all business areas. Develop knowledge and relationships across the industry.
Drive product adoption of The Orchard’s tools and services across client base.
Represent The Orchard at events and conferences as required.
Your Skills and Experience:: Minimum 2 years relevant experience in a music distributor, label or management environment
A strong understanding of digital distribution, metadata requirements and digital marketing.
Highly experienced in the creation and distribution life cycle of physical releases and associated deadlines from production through release date.
Established relationships with a variety of publicists, press and radio companies.
Highly advanced organisational skills with the ability to multitask and prioritise to tight deadlines.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to speak effectively across all levels of business and the capacity to identify key relationships and develop them accordingly.
A willingness to approach new areas of business, with a positive and considered attitude.
Awareness of changes and developments in the industry, with particular emphasis on competitor activity.
Strong industry network and relationships within the independent label space.
Fluent in English, additional languages desirable but not essential.
The ideal candidate is:: Patient and empathetic with all clients from label owners and artist managers to interns.
Friendly, approachable and enthusiastic with an innate desire to understand the needs of clients.
A brilliant all round communicator – on phone, email and in person.
Someone with supreme attention to detail and follow-through skills.
Resourceful and takes the initiative – an expert troubleshooter.
Passionate about music and the music industry with drive and enthusiasm to excel.

Title: Royalties Assistant (UK)

Weâre trusted by some of the worldâs biggest names like Childish Gambino, Akon, Freya Ridings, Paul McCartney and The Red Hot Chili Peppers and represent more than 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in the U.S. and U.K.We thrive on creativity and are committed to championing diversity. Title: Copyright & Contract Administrator (US)
Description: Primary Wave Music is one of the largest independent music publishing and talent management companies in the United States, founded in 2006.Primary Wave Music creates marketing and branding strategies for its diverse roster of iconic and legendary artists and songs through their four in-house operating divisions: Music Publishing, Talent Management and Brand and Digital agencies, to help cross promote our artists and brand relationships. We are committed to artistry, innovation and entrepreneurship.With just three employees in 2006, Primary Wave has grown to a company of 70+ employees today, with offices in New York, Los Angeles, London, and Austin. With a team of the industryâs finest executives, Primary Wave is consistently growing and looking towards the future, focusing on integrating all aspects of the entertainment industry.Copyright & Contract AdministratorWe are looking for an enthusiastic colleague with a dedicated work ethic and a sharp mind who will be responsible for copyright royalty administration, contract management, and statement production.This position will ensure that all contract, copyright and asset management data is ingested into internal and external systems accurately and timely and that all obligations to report to third parties are met.This position will play an integral role in the development and maintenance of systems designed to collect and organize data around catalogs, assets, and transactions. The role reports to the Director of Copyrights & Royalties and will work very closely with the CFO and COO.Your specific responsibilities will include, but will not be limited to:Ensuring that data is accurately summarized from contracts and recorded into internal documents and systems; facilitating cross-functional awareness to and understanding of all dealsPreparing and delivering timely and accurate statements and payments to writers and partners reflecting contract termsTracking advance recoupment and liaising with creative departments to ensure awareness of deal statusTracking licensing approval requirements and liaising with synch licensing department to ensure awarenessManaging the new deal onboarding checklist to ensure all pre- and post-closing steps are achievedDelivering deals and song data to administrators; facilitating catalog transfer between societies and other publishersWorking with the Director of Copyrights & Royalties to communicate with writers regarding their royalties, registrations, and any claim disputesHelping to manage royalty audits, including sending notification letters and communicating with engaged auditorsActing as the lead contact with partners to ensure a successful implementation of a contract, royalties and asset management database system.Managing the accuracy and completeness of the database and catalog asset tracking system on an ongoing basisHelping to ensure compliance with all internal and external policies and regulationsReviewing workflows and driving continuous process improvements; acting as a change agent to increase operational effectivenessCommunicating regularly and effectively with the team to raise concerns and ensure transparencyAs the ideal candidate, you will have:Bachelorâs degree required, with 5+ years of relevant work experience in music or related fieldMinimum of 2 years of work experience with music publishing contracts and copyright administrationUnderstanding of worldwide publishing and copyright royalties requiredSome accounting and/or finance experience requiredStrong knowledge of and experience with software technology and data manipulationAbility to synthesize data and create reports from scratchExceptional attention to detail and accuracyAbility to multi-task and manage time to prioritize tasksMust be self-motivated with an intellectual curiosityExcellent analytical and problem-solving skillsStrong verbal and written communication skillsBenefits:We work to maintain the best possible environment for our employees, where people can learn and grow with the company. We strive to provide a collaborative, creative environment where each person feels encouraged to contribute to our processes, decisions, planning and culture.To Apply:To respond to this great opportunity, please go to: http://ejob.bz/ATS/jb.do?reqGK=27377828We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Title: Sync Manager (US)

Experience of staff management, supervision, motivation and training.

Excellent organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines in an often highly pressured environment.
Experience of delivering financial information and statistical data.

Title: Income Tracking Manager (UK)
Description: With its broad and diverse roster of new stars and legendary artists, Warner Music Group is home to a collection of the best-known record labels in the music industry including Asylum, Atlantic, East West, Elektra, FFRR, Fueled by Ramen, Nonesuch, Parlophone, Rhino, Roadrunner, Sire, Warner Bros., Warner Classics and Warner/Chappell Music, one of the world’s leading music publishers with a catalogue of more than one million copyrights worldwide.For more than four decades, WMG has been an industry-leading force in providing a world-class array of services designed to help artists and labels grow their careers and their businesses.Artist & Label Services is the umbrella for WEA (Warner-Elektra-Atlantic) – the pioneering WEA distribution and marketing network – and Alternative Distribution Alliance (ADA) – the ground-breaking global distribution company for independent artists and labels.Job Title: Income Tracking ManagerA little bit about our team:The Income Tracking Manager role falls within the overall structure of the Administration Team. The role reports directly to the UK Head of Administration, but will liaise with all areas of our business and work closely with our Finance, Royalty, Copyright, Global Analytics and Creative/Sync teams and you will have the opportunity to work internationally with affiliates, licensees and collection societies.Why this could be your next big break:We are Warner Chappell Music. Where the songwriters are heard. As the UK Income Tracking Manager you will lead all tracking activities for our UK and multi-territorial digital deals. You will have the opportunity to define the role, create a blueprint for how we manage our income tracking activities and create processes that can easily adapat to the constantly evolving nature of music licensing.You will be responsible for the forensic analysis of income received against income expectations across all major revenue streams and instigate backclaims, adjustments and supplemental payments where required. You will enjoy the opportunity to provide input into the creation of reports that support income tracking, including exception & variance reporting, as required for the UK business.Here you’ll get to:Lead all income tracking efforts for the UK business, including our multi-territorial digital licensing PEDL deals.Create new processes and ways of working to take our income tracking to the next level.Work as integrated member of a future-facing administration team.Provide the business with regular forecasts/updates by income providers (society/DSP/ affiliate/third party).You will monitor accurate deal level expectations across all income streams and provide income confirmations to the Finance & Royalty teams.Idenitify and make claims for shortfalls in income.Liaise with counterparts across all affiliate offices, suppliers and sub-publishers to ensure income flows are in line with expectations & resolve any interruptions or discrepancies.Ensure affiliate income queries relating to domestic income are resolvedWork with our international PEDL processing partners and represent the company to third parties, licensees, collection societies and industry bodiesTake a leading role in establishing forecast mechanisms, in line with the requirements of our finance & royalty teams.You will work closely with the UK Royalty and Copyright teams to both resolve and proactively prevent against problems arising with the collection of incomeAbout you:An experienced royalty tracker (minimum 2+ years experience) who has worked across all major revenue streams, including film /TV/radio broadcasts/ live performance, plus physical and digital sales.In-depth knowledge of PRS/MCPS licensing schemes and distribution policies.Skills to analyse, extract & manipulate data from high volume datafiles.Experience in setting up internal analysis/forecasting delivery reports tailored to the needs of the business.A solid understanding of the digital supply chain and the relationship steps necessary to facilitate multi-territory licensing.A forensic and detail-oriented approach to analysing data with a track record in delivering accurate resultsYou have advanced Excel capabilities including Power Pivot & advanced formulaeYou have proficiency with SQL.You have a detailed knowledge of industry and DSP file formats, such as, CRD, CSV,DIF,FLAT,TEXT & society specific bespoke reporting formats.It would be music to our ears if you also had:Confidence in clearly communicating with clients, colleagues and managementExcellent organisational and time management skillsThe ability to manage multiple projects at once and hit time-sensistive deadlinesThe ability to demonstrate your personal drive and ambitionThe ability to join dots, think laterally and understand both downstream and upstream impact of eventA thorough understanding of international collection society structures and their licensing/distribution processesExperience of working for a digital service provider or related digital music partnerA passion for music.A background in a finance or royalty tracking.Why join us?Here at Warner Music Group we’re all about our people. We are one global company made up exclusively of knowledgeable, passionate, and creative people.It is the mission of every member of the WMG team around the world to create a nurturing environment for artists, songwriters, and the people behind the music – at every stage of their career. We strive to set WMG apart by embracing a philosophy of innovation – it’s part of our company’s DNA.Consider a career at WMG and be a part of one of the most influential forces in culture today.Love this job and want to apply?Click the “Apply” link at the top of the page, or apply directly with your LinkedIn. Applying with LinkedIn will import all of the information you put in your profile, but will still allow you to upload a resume and cover letter.Don’t be discouraged if you don’t hear from us right away. We love it here, and think you will, too.We are an equal opportunity employer that is committed to inclusion and diversity and will not discriminate against any applicant or employee on the basis of age, sex, sexual orientation, race, color, creed, religion, ethnicity, national origin, alienage or citizenship, disability, marital status, familial status, military or veteran status, or any other legal recognized protected basis under federal, state or local laws, regulations or ordinances.We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. If you need a reasonable accommodation because of a disability for any part of the application or employment process, please send an e-mail to Recruitment@wmg.com and let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.Copyright © 2019 Warner Music Inc.

Title: Junior Artist Manager (UK)
Description: We are looking for a proactive and passionate music industry professional to become part of a small team that represents both internationally established and rising artists / producers.The position will be split between artist management and heading up our in-house record label, predominantly managing artist campaign strategies and objectives. It will be a flexible role that includes, but is not necessarily limited to, the responsibilities listed below.The ideal candidate will have experience in a similar role and should be willing to travel and work remotely.RESPONSIBILITIES Take a hands-on approach to the planning and execution of artist single & album campaigns, including release schedules, touring, marketing, promo and press. An in-depth knowledge of the music industry and passion for music, specifically indie/alternative. Strong understanding of social media, marketing tools and trends.Please send a CV and cover email to musicpajobapplication@gmail.com Deadline:Monday 9th MarchSalaryNegotiable, dependent on experience.

Title: Artist Marketing Lead, Music Marketing (US)
Description: Spotify has transformed the way people access and enjoy music. Today, millions of people in 79 countries and territories have access to more than 40 million tracks, whenever and wherever they want. At Spotify, we’re focused on providing the right music experience for every moment.The Music Marketing team plays a critical role in defining and driving Spotify’s promotional strategy around new and existing content, focused on the holistic music experience. As part of the Music Marketing team you will help build and shape the marketing processes, cross functional infrastructure, operations and implementing innovation and best practices across new album releases, individual artist and industry moments and Spotify’s original and programmed artist content.This position requires experience and passion for the music and entertainment industry with a firm pulse across a wide range of genres and its cultural impact. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be brilliant. Title: Content Editor (UK)
Description: Content Editor – Communications & MarketingWe have an exciting opportunity for a Content Editor to join our Communications & Marketing team working across our Kings Cross & Streatham offices. About UsPRS for Music Limited is the UK’s leading collection society, bringing together two collection societies: the Mechanical-Copyright Protection Society and the Performing Right Society.We undertake collective rights management for musical works on behalf of over 140,000 members. We pay royalties to our members when their work is performed, broadcast, streamed, downloaded, reproduced, played in public or used in film and TV. We support them by influencing policy, supporting and hosting awards and events, and investing in new technology to ensure we’re fit for the digital music age.About the RoleAre you as passionate about music as you are about crafting great content?We’re looking for an experienced Content Editor with a flair for creating engaging copy and rich media to play an integral role in our Creative Services team.You’ll be producing daily content for PRS for Music’s online channels, including M online and its associated social media platforms, and will assist with the production of high-quality video and audio content, including live events and interviews.You’ll also provide a copywriting and proof-reading resource for the wider Marketing and Communications department, writing adverts and marketing materials, and supporting the overall work of the department.The role of Content Editor will include:Pitching, sourcing and creating daily written content for M online including business news, interviews and featuresCreating and uploading content for M’s Instagram and Twitter accounts with a view to grow both channelsUploading and sharing M content using Content Management Systems, ensuring that the editorial calendar is filledCreating and updating written content for PRS for Music print collateral, online marketing, website copy and brand messagingAssisting with the production of video and audio content, including interviews, live music sessions and playlistsAttending and reporting from industry conferences and events, representing both M and PRS for MusicWorking with the Editorial Manager to ensure communications adhere to tone of voice and style guidelinesThe salary for this role is circa £28,000 per annum, depending on experience.About YouThe successful applicant will have an understanding of what makes engaging and informative copy with a proven track record for creating top quality journalistic content.You will be a confident, experienced writer or journalist with a natural aptitude for writing clear and concise content for a variety of audiences. You will also be compliant with best practice SEO.Ideally you will have a background working within a busy magazine, communications, media or agency environment. Experience of creating rich media content including audio and visual assets, preferably using Final Cut Pro for the Mac, is also desirable for this role.Why PRS?PRS for Music is a forward-thinking music copyright company with a focus on being the number one rights management company in the world.We live by our values they are deeply ingrained in our company culture. We want to help you grow throughout your career, so we have plenty of development opportunities for you.We run a number of well-being activities throughout the year and encourage flexible working and a relaxed dress code.We also have a number of perks including roof terraces on both sites, regular live music events, subsidised cafes, corporate discounts including gym membership and home working.Next StepsDoes this sound like you? Title: UK Sales Manager (UK)
Description: BMAT started in 2005 when a group of engineers with a passion for music decided to spin-off from an audio research lab in Barcelona.The company started with an idea – to index all the music in the world – and a mission – to better the music eco-system – and a team of nerds eager to learn how to build a successful business.Today the company has grown to more than a hundred people – spread across Barcelona, Buenos Aires, Lima, São Paulo, London, Paris, Oslo, Tel-Aviv, Johannesburg, Singapore, Tokyo and Seoul – that team up to deliver daily 1 million identifications and 3 billion matches to our partners from all over the music industry.Our CultureWe love music. It makes our lives a zillion times better. And we want to give something back to her (yes, to us music is a lady).Make her life easier and her future brighter. We apply our scientific, data-infused brains to tell everyone what music has been played – so that creators get the recognition they deserve. We hear everything everywhere and tell everyone who wants to know. ‘What plays around will come back around’ we say. We believe by helping this happen we can make a positive change in the music ecosystem and enjoy talent for another million years.We are a team of pretty decent people who enjoy working with each other. We’re open-minded and well-traveled, coming from all parts of the world. We like coffee, tea, chai, and mate all the same. We’ve got rappers, producers, cello players, DJs, and songwriters. The passion for music, the excitement for technology and Barcelona as a backdrop has proven to make a good combination. We say there is a BMAT touch. We believe it has to do with being passionate, obsessive, nice, generous, honest, self-motivated, entrepreneurial, intrepid, crazy, extraordinary, creative, agile, human, friendly, smart, witty, talented, naive, optimistic, surprising, sensual, and nerdy, but not very hipster (unfortunately).Sales at BMATSales is a core team at the BMAT compound of regional managers, business vice-presidents, and executive directors. Its dynamic is very open, co-operative and no hierarchical. We understand sales as a discovery process through which, based on our expertise, we ask and listen our customers telling us about their challenges and in return we advise them on how we can help them tackle those through innovation.What You Will Be DoingUnderstand the newest needs/requests and problems faced by the Music and Audiovisual industries to propose relevant services.Provide accurate and regular forecasts on the UK market to the business development team.Define product strategy together with Global Sales management.Develop relationships with the industry to generate new business development opportunities for our five verticals – TVAV, Broadcast, Digital, Venues, Charts.Spot and answer tender calls.Take responsibility for the UK Sales P&L.All About YouMinimum 10 years experience in an international Sales facing role within a music and/ or technology company.The ideal applicant will have a strong network and a good understanding of international copyrights.Experience managing a sales division within Music publishing/ Production music/ Film & TV/Synch is valued.Superior analytical and communication skills.Fluency in English.Bonus TrackAvailability for training in our Barcelona HQ.This role may require travels.You Can EnjoyFull-time job.Flexibility on working hours.Flat hierarchy.Full autonomy of your dutiesBecoming part of a diverse and multicultural team based in Barcelona obsessed with bettering the music industry.Diverse perspectives drive innovation, and innovation is kind of our thing. Our open-minded and flexible culture organically fosters this diversity. We’ve learnt that we thrive on learning from each other, so we don’t just welcome but actively hire a range of open-minded people from all walks of life. Our team is made up of people with different backgrounds, perspectives, orientations, abilities, musical tastes, dress sense and time-zones – but we all have one common goal: Making the music world a better place. With equal opportunities for everyone, BMAT is a pretty nice place to work.

Our Culture: We love music. It makes our lives a zillion times better. And we want to give something back to her (yes, to us music is a lady).
What You Will Be Doing: Understand the newest needs/requests and problems faced by the Music and Audiovisual industries to propose relevant services.
Provide accurate and regular forecasts on the UK market to the business development team.
Define product strategy together with Global Sales management.
Develop relationships with the industry to generate new business development opportunities for our five verticals – TVAV, Broadcast, Digital, Venues, Charts.
Take responsibility for the UK Sales P&L.
All About You: Minimum 10 years experience in an international Sales facing role within a music and/ or technology company.
The ideal applicant will have a strong network and a good understanding of international copyrights.
Experience managing a sales division within Music publishing/ Production music/ Film & TV/Synch is valued.
Bonus Track: Availability for training in our Barcelona HQ.
You Can Enjoy: Becoming part of a diverse and multicultural team based in Barcelona obsessed with bettering the music industry.

Title: UK Head of Marketing (UK)

Title: Operations Administrator (US)

Description: Secretly Group Seeks Project Manager (North America) for Bloomington (IN), Brooklyn (NY), or Los Angeles (CA).  Position Summary Secretly Group is looking for a motivated, ambitious and enthusiastic full-time Project Manager. The position will be based in one of our 3 main US offices: Bloomington (IN), Brooklyn (NY), Los Angeles (CA). 2-4 years of music industry experience are essential. The candidate must have a passion for music, a knack for organization, be keen to contribute to the global strategy for our projects, and an ability to communicate effectively with artists and managers.Company Summary Secretly Group is made up of the Dead Oceans, Jagjaguwar, and Secretly Canadian record labels. We are headquartered in Bloomington, IN, with offices in Brooklyn, London, and Los Angeles, and additional staff in Paris, Berlin, Amsterdam, and beyond. We release music by artists such as Mitski, Khruangbin, Phoebe Bridgers, Bon Iver, Moses Sumney, Angel Olsen, Whitney, serpentwithfeet, and Yoko Ono. Principle Responsibilities Work closely with A&R and serve as the primary operational point of contact and advocate for releasesMaintain and foster relationships with artists providing an active and available label voice/presence alongside A&R.Manage project budgets, label copy, sales goals, production deadlines, and campaign timelines.Utilize data analytics to guide strategic decisions.Oversee all core elements of the record release cycle. Help to coordinate assets across campaigns, including music videos, remixes, live sessions, photoshoots, digital assets, etc.Liaise with internal label staff (marketing, promotions, radio, production, distribution, etc), and 3rd party team members (publicity, booking agents, sync placement, creatives, etc) to assure all parties have the information, resources, and access they require to excel in their work. At the same time, liaise with artists and management to both provide access to the wider team while also making that team feel manageable.The role is full-time, and will require some out-of-office-hours activity, such as attendance at shows, accompanying artists on promotional activities, etc.Required SkillsExcellent communication and organizational skills are essential.Must possess strong problem solving and analytical skills.Must be able to demonstrate independent thought and action.Ability to set priorities and meet deadlines.Effectively manage multiple concurrent projects.Background of 2-4 years in the music industry.BenefitsHealth, Dental, Vision, and Life insurance401KPaid holidaysPaid vacation daysContact: To apply or to recommend a qualified candidate, send an email directly to Jessie LaJoie, Secretly’s HR Director at applications@secretlyservices.comAll qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to disability, protected veteran status, age, race, creed, color, religion, sex, marital status, affectional or sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, ancestry, nationality, or national origin.

Title: Executive Assistant (UK)
Description: We are a rapidly expanding Entertainment company with offices in London and USA.This is a full-time permanent role based in our London office, and with an immediate start.Salary: competitiveInclusion in our company pension scheme and healthcare benefits after satisfactory probation period.Assisting the Group Director, you will work across all projects he is involved with.Duties will include:Managing his diary in Microsoft Outlook, working across different time zonesOrganising travel to USA or other destinations for businessStructuring internal company meetings and preparing meeting agendasAttending internal meetings and taking notes – following up action pointsManaging documents for filing on our cloud systemAssist in all legal and financial obligationsPrepare PowerPoint presentationsReconciling invoices and receiptsAssist in ensuring clear and regular communication to all personnelThe company portfolio is currently expanding so there may be further duties added to the above and the ability for the right candidate to progress in this position.You will be fully versed in the Microsoft package for Mac; Word, Excel, PowerPoint and OneDrive. The Group Director’s diary is constantly changing, so good organisational skills are key to keeping on top of this.Our environment is constantly evolving, and the company is currently undergoing positive growth. The ideal candidate will be very motivated and pro-active with the ability to be flexible and show initiative. Calmness and a logical approach are important.The person we are looking for will be naturally energetic and hard working. The role would suit someone who has been in a similar Executive role for a few years and looking to take on more responsibility.While the company promotes respecting staff personal time, the role will require calls/emails during weekends or evenings and the person we are looking for will be used to occasional out of hours calls.Due to the high volume of applicants, we will not be able to respond to all unsuccessful applicants.NO AGENCIES.

Duties will include:: Managing his diary in Microsoft Outlook, working across different time zones
Organising travel to USA or other destinations for business
Structuring internal company meetings and preparing meeting agendas
Attending internal meetings and taking notes – following up action points
Managing documents for filing on our cloud system
Assist in all legal and financial obligations
Assist in ensuring clear and regular communication to all personnel

Title: Creative Manager, Apple Music, EMEA (UK)
Description: People at Apple don’t just design products — they design the kind of wonder that’s revolutionized entire industries. It’s the diversity of those people and their ideas that inspires the innovation that runs through everything we do.Apple is looking for an outstanding creative leader for the Music Content Design team across Europe, The Middle East and Africa. This is a role for a true creative.A design thinker, communicator and problem solver who wants to have their hands in several things at once; who can conceptualize across screens, formats and break the conventions of those formats.Someone who can passionately lead a global team and is dedicated to shaping lasting meaningful editorial experiences for our listeners.Key QualificationsExtensive experience in design and creative leadership required; experience with music labels, publishing, magazines or editorial content creation.A creative thinker who is strategically-centered, organized and process driven.Fluency across graphic design, typography, photography, motion and illustration.Excellent communication, both verbal and nonverbal.Have willingness to work on a global team, across PST and GMT time zones is required.A deep understanding on how brands scale along with how they communicate and shift within varying applications.Passion for cultivating relationships, mentoring creatives and the ability to use that rapport to align teams around a vision.Able to give concise and well considered creative direction.Ability to create and communicate comprehensive presentations around an idea, the team’s work, a campaign or future initiatives.Experienced in developing and executing an editorial design strategy and an advanced understanding of what makes content engaging and successful.Passionate about fostering a collaborative and inspiring team that will interface with multiple facets of Design at Apple.Keep up with and inspire from the evolving landscape of available music, technology and art.Strong photographic sensibility; sourcing, art-buying, editing, re-touching or capturing.A keen ability to create work under minimal supervision in an international context, as well as package and present solutions to the wider creative team.Act as a cultural guide, exposing unique, regional visual trends throughout EMEA markets and seamlessly applying them to the Apple brand.Collaborate with Creatives, Project Managers, Business Owners and Executives to solve complex problems with simple solutions.Demonstrated deep knowledge of Adobe Creative and iWork suites.DescriptionThe Content Design team conceptualizes, designs and creates editorial content for some of Apple’s most visible products. Your ideas will impact the way we represent music and how we form visual conversations with listeners.You will lead creative strategy on how Apple Music looks and feels for listeners across the globe.You’ll have a deep abiding love for music along with the artwork and the visual culture around it.A proven ability to deliver designs across multiple platforms that authentically represent the content.Your knowledge in music will range from contemporary to classics to regional deep cuts and you will be naturally eager to continue to learn.You will have proven experience taking calculated risks that drive culture, have an extraordinary eye for detail, a keen sense of what matters and a belief that you can make a difference.This is a leadership role that requires collaboration across multiple design, business and production teams.This person will lead the team responsible for creating original playlist artwork, campaign artwork and featuring artwork that will appear on Apple Music and iTunes Store.Additional RequirementsBachelor’s degree in Graphic, Information, Communication, Web design or equivalent experiencePortfolio demonstrating proficiency in delivering strategic ideas across digital, social, motion and/or static form factors.Passionate about the Apple brand and committed in preserving its authenticityWillingness to travel to EMEA markets which Apple operates services in at least once per year.English fluency a requirement. Additional languages are beneficial.

A creative thinker who is strategically-centered, organized and process driven.

Excellent communication, both verbal and nonverbal.

Keep up with and inspire from the evolving landscape of available music, technology and art.

Title: Royalties Assistant (UK)
Description: Lime Blue Music is a rapidly growing London based company that specialises in the worldwide management, collection and distribution of Neighbouring Rights, one of the fastest growing revenue streams in music.Working primarily within the electronic, pop & urban scenes, our goal is to maximise Neighbouring Rights income for our clients through our direct relationships with collection societies, detailed data through our bespoke online portal, and rapid accounting.We are seeking a self-motivated and driven individual to join our day to day operation to assist with royalty processing to ensure we are maximising revenue for both our artists and labels.RoleCorresponding with both our collection societies (CMO’s) worldwide and our clients assuring all statements are correct and successfully imported into our bespoke system.Ensuring that societies are sending timely statements and following up when necessaryReconciling society statementsSimple preparation of invoices where necessaryInvestigating incorrect royalty statements & analysing statements for missing recordingsWorking with our team to strict deadlines to ensure clients’ statements are accurate and completeRegular meetings and reports to managementOther similar royalty administration tasks that we would require your assistance with.Skills / Qualifications:Royalty administration experienceWell-rounded knowledge of the music industry and experience in royalty processingHighly organised & time management skills are a must.Excellent written and oral communication skillsMust be proficient with Mac OS and ExcelAble to work with large amounts of data.Ability to work to tight monthly deadlinesQuick learnerGet in touch:Please send your CV and covering letter via email to jobs@limebluemusic.com and please outline the role you’re applying for in the email subject.Salary competitive but dependent on experience.

Role: Corresponding with both our collection societies (CMO’s) worldwide and our clients assuring all statements are correct and successfully imported into our bespoke system.
Ensuring that societies are sending timely statements and following up when necessary
Simple preparation of invoices where necessary
Investigating incorrect royalty statements & analysing statements for missing recordings
Working with our team to strict deadlines to ensure clients’ statements are accurate and complete
Regular meetings and reports to management
Other similar royalty administration tasks that we would require your assistance with.
Skills / Qualifications:: Well-rounded knowledge of the music industry and experience in royalty processing
Highly organised & time management skills are a must.
Excellent written and oral communication skills
Must be proficient with Mac OS and Excel
Able to work with large amounts of data.
Ability to work to tight monthly deadlines
Get in touch:: Please send your CV and covering letter via email to jobs@limebluemusic.com and please outline the role you’re applying for in the email subject.

About Merlin: Merlin, the digital licensing hub for the world’s independent music sector, commands over 15% of the global recorded music market. Merlin offers digital music services and emerging technology companies the means to efficiently license the world’s most commercially successful independent music. This enables Merlin’s members to more effectively capitalize on revenue from these services, while ensuring their repertoire is appropriately valued.

Processing membership applications and communications with applicants through our on-boarding process;

Liaise with the project coordinator on IT issues and development.

Gather and maintain member data relative to maximizing opportunities for members with DSP partners in relation to new releases/catalogues;

Work alongside Merlin Member Services & Operations teams to maintain Participating Member lists as they relate to the operational aspects of Merlin deals;
Training and certification on rights management and other DSP tools to assist in practical member support;
Coordinate scheduling and admin of member workshops and presentations;
Manage inbound Merlin Member Services & Operations email accounts;
Assist with set-up and maintenance of Merlin-DSP pitching and participation forms;
Assist Merlin team on admin tasks relating to maximizing participation across the membership;
Manage error tickets and other notices of operational requirements from DSPs;
Coordinate project management for member facing IT issues and tools developments;
Core skills:: 2 or more years of experience in a client facing support role;
Excellent math, verbal and written communication skills (English; multilingual is a plus);
Exceptional attention to detail and well organized;
Skilled listener in order to accurately respond to inquiries and requests from within a global reaching organization and team;

Proficiency with computer applications including Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint), Apple Products (KeyNote), Google Applications Suite (Google sheets, docs etc) and various task management applications;
Comfortable tracking and prioritizing multiple issues across different platforms;
Able to work under pressure in a busy office environment;
Comfortable communicating to staff across all levels via email and, where applicable, via phone and in person;
Comfortable assisting staff, where required, in meetings (both internal and external).
Advanced skills:: Previous experience working with a member-based organization (distributor, collecting society, trade association);
Knowledge of the digital music industry;
Knowledge of/interest in independent music, Hip-Hop and/or Latin music genres;
Experience in digital service provider operations;
Understanding of music rights and their stakeholders;
Proficiency with rights management / content recognition tools;
Understanding of social media marketing methods and tools;

You will be responsible for supporting the International Finance team within its worldwide hub.Our recordings hub manages the financial and management reporting for the global recording business for revenues and costs and provides key business support.Your Responsibilities.SAP support including Journal posting and preparationAssist with preparation of Balance Sheet RecsMonth end file roll forwardSupport mapping table review for new projects and missing codesSupport with IC matrices; ADA revenue & cost analysis, Quarterly Intercompany matrix, Monthly Cost recharges, Quarterly PPI (all to be turned around in tight bottleneck time periods and require manual analysis/processing)Review of G/Ls looking for posting errors (including mid-month reviews)Support with BW report preparation and reviewAd-hoc data queries and wider support to the team as requiredYour Profile.Minimum 2 years experience working in a similar Finance positionPrevious experience in a similar role within the music industry preferred but not essentialPart Qualified AccountantProficient in excel (intermediate level)SAP experience preferableCommitment to continual professional self-developmentTeam playerMethodicalHighly organisedPassion for music, of course!Now, what’s in it for you?25 days annual leave plus 3 days between Christmas & New YearSubsidised gym membershipPrivate Health InsuranceCompetitive pension schemeAnnual gig allowanceArtist showcasesCycle to work schemeSeason Ticket LoanAccess to our Employee Assistance ProgrammeExcellent development opportunitiesA fun and sociable office environmentWhat you should know about us.BMG is a growing international music company, founded in 2008 with a revolutionary vision for the digital age. Title: Digital Marketing Manager (UK)
Description: The Digital Marketing Manager’s role will focus on all digital aspects of an artist and product release – balancing both creative and commercial objectives through the setting and achieving of campaign-specific objectives & KPIs.They will create and execute regularly updated social media plans and be responsible for preparing reports to inform management and label of strategy moving forward.Working closely with artists, artist management, label colleagues in Marketing, PR, A&R as well as key external partners – i.e. Twitter, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, Spotify etc in order to gain maximum exposure for artists, build fan bases and deliver on release strategies.About Nettwerk Nettwerk is a record label, artist management company, and music publisher headquartered in Vancouver, Canada, with offices in Los Angeles, New York, London, and Hamburg. We’re collaborative and innovative music lovers aiming to create long, sustainable careers for our clients by building deep and personal connections to their fans. We’re looking for inspired, skilled and energetic people to help us deliver on that promise.We work with over 100 clients across a variety of genres, with the overarching philosophy to only work with artists that create music that we love.Main Duties Promotion:Creating innovative content strategies to maximize existing content, create content and propose original commissions Devising and implementing best in class, audience-led social media strategies, driving fan audience growth and ongoing engagement across platforms using insights, best practices and tapping into content trends Identifying emerging content, tech and social trends and infuse artist campaigns with creative applications of new technology Determining and applying correct tone of voice, content and branding for each artist, using agreed artist vision brief Amplify all elements of wider campaign – marketing, promo, etc. – across artist channelsEstablishing and overseeing approval processes with management/artist/within the labelAnalysing campaigns and artist specific data with a range of other platforms/tools to analyse audience-related and performance data, creating meaningful reports that inform the wider labelWeekly report creation of assigned artists streaming figuresManaging and creating mailers for direct marketingCreating online paid advertising for the UK/EU markets to maximize organic marketing where appropriateUploading artist content across all social networksUnderstanding specific traits of various UK/EU markets to best fit digital marketing for maximum effectivenessCreating and maintaining direct relationships with all key existing DSP’s and emerging platformsCopy writing for artist websites/website upkeepStay up to date on key trends, pop culture, new social media platform features, and marketing technology to ensure all campaigns are relevant and cutting-edge Budgets:Abide by the budgets being set by the Product Manager in respect to the promotions activated for Nettwerk artists.Track digital spend for each artist so they can tally with the bigger marketing budget set by the Product Manager. Collaboration/Teamwork:The Digital Manager will be part of a small dynamic team in the London office, working in tandem with Nettwerk’s international teams; it is important they have a positive willingness to dedicate themself to making the team work internally in the UK, and within the larger global Nettwerk team.The Digital Manager will be involved in weekly calls with the North American and European Nettwerk offices, to discuss the progress and roll out of content and artist releases.Requirements: Qualified candidates for this position must have: 1-2 years digital promotion experience; University degree preferred; Passion for music! Music industry experience; Experience with Photoshop, HTML, and Adobe Premiere Pro; Portfolio of work required; Strong marketing strategy & development skills; willingness to constantly explore new platforms and opportunities; In depth experience with social media platforms, analytics, steaming and mobile services and apps Experience with email marketing management; Sense of urgency and commitment to meeting deadlines; Strong communication skills, both verbal and written; Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work with many different departments/personalities; Thrives in a team environment; Ability to work well under pressure and multi-task in a fast-paced environment; Outstanding organizational skills; Tech-savvy.Please send cover letter, CV and portfolio to jobs@nettwerk.com, with the subject line: Digital Marketing Manager Role. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.
