and was named a leading digital advisory agency to the entertainment industry by the Sunday Times in 2019.Their client list includes internationally renowned artists, industry-leading management companies, both major and independent record labels, emerging talent and stretches to some of the biggest names in UK touring and festivals.Heading into an exciting year as an agency, Deviate is looking to expand its vibrant and talented Digital Marketing Team to match the growing demand for its forward-thinking approach to digital marketing in the music industry.JOB DESCRIPTIONManage social media campaigns, timelines and create comprehensive social media plans to support world-leading digital marketing campaigns.Produce and implement ‘on-brand’ social copy and content for clients and artists.Assist Digital Marketing Managers in building and maintaining ad-buying and digital marketing campaigns.Research key audiences for new artists and produce detailed targeting reports to be used in digital advertising.Delve into new digital trends and research how they could be implemented for clients campaigns.Produce creative assets for use in social media and advertising campaigns.Take on day-to-day admin tasks as required.WHO YOU ARESomeone with a real passion for music and tech.Confident in using adobe creative suite packages to produce simple, high-quality assets.A background in music with good knowledge of the release process, touring and how the industry works.Solid copywriting ability.Organized self-starter with the capacity to manage an often intense workload and work in a high-pressure environment.Values working collaboratively but knows how to work independently to great effect.Excellent communication skills.Ambition to continuously improve and approach each campaign with enthusiasm and innovative/creative mind-set.To apply for this role please send your cv and cover letter to jobs@deviate.digital

JOB DESCRIPTION: Manage social media campaigns, timelines and create comprehensive social media plans to support world-leading digital marketing campaigns.
Produce and implement ‘on-brand’ social copy and content for clients and artists.
WHO YOU ARE: Someone with a real passion for music and tech.
Confident in using adobe creative suite packages to produce simple, high-quality assets.
A background in music with good knowledge of the release process, touring and how the industry works.
Solid copywriting ability.
Organized self-starter with the capacity to manage an often intense workload and work in a high-pressure environment.
Values working collaboratively but knows how to work independently to great effect.
Excellent communication skills.
Ambition to continuously improve and approach each campaign with enthusiasm and innovative/creative mind-set.

Title: Digital Marketing Manager (UK)
Description: Deviate Digital are seeking an experienced Digital Marketing Manager to join their growing team based at Tileyard Studios in Kings Cross, London. Deviate Digital was founded by Sammy Andrews in 2017 and was named a leading digital advisory agency to the entertainment industry by the Sunday Times in 2019.Their client list includes internationally renowned artists, industry-leading management companies, both major and independent record labels, emerging talent and stretches to some of the biggest names in UK touring and festivals.Heading into an exciting year as an agency, Deviate is looking to expand its vibrant and talented Digital Marketing Team to match growing demand for their forward-thinking approach to digital marketing in the music industry.JOB DESCRIPTIONProvide world-leading digital marketing campaigns, streaming strategies, advertising & social media strategies, using experience and expertise to formulate original and groundbreaking ideas.Set up and manage digital advertising campaigns ensuring spends are effectively maximized to achieve campaign goals.Work with our creative department to provide content and assets as and when required for social media and advertising campaigns.Assess and strategise the digital presence of artists across all digital platforms.Advise and consult with clients on digital strategy during the pre/post-release cycle.Analyse data from various sources both in house and external to create insight reports and reactive marketing.Critically analyse and report on digital campaign and ad performance.Maintain and develop relationships with key digital service providers, social media platforms and digital media outlets.WHO YOU AREYou have 3-5 years’ relevant experience.You enjoy future-gazing and trend watching and have a true passion for music and tech.You have a solid grounding in music marketing and experience building and implementing digital campaigns.You have a firm understanding of the current landscape in the music industry and are eager to innovate within it.You have proven experience working across the multitude of digital platforms available.Proven track record delivering results and KPI’s through both organic and paid campaigns.Organized self-starter with the capacity to manage an often intense workload and work in a high-pressure environment.Values working collaboratively but knows how to work independently to great effect.Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.Ambition to continuously improve and approach each campaign with enthusiasm and innovative/creative mind-set.To apply for this role please send your cv and cover letter to jobs@deviate.digital

JOB DESCRIPTION: Provide world-leading digital marketing campaigns, streaming strategies, advertising & social media strategies, using experience and expertise to formulate original and groundbreaking ideas.
Set up and manage digital advertising campaigns ensuring spends are effectively maximized to achieve campaign goals.
Work with our creative department to provide content and assets as and when required for social media and advertising campaigns.
Assess and strategise the digital presence of artists across all digital platforms.
Advise and consult with clients on digital strategy during the pre/post-release cycle.
Analyse data from various sources both in house and external to create insight reports and reactive marketing.
Critically analyse and report on digital campaign and ad performance.
Maintain and develop relationships with key digital service providers, social media platforms and digital media outlets.
WHO YOU ARE: You have 3-5 years’ relevant experience.
You enjoy future-gazing and trend watching and have a true passion for music and tech.
You have a solid grounding in music marketing and experience building and implementing digital campaigns.
You have a firm understanding of the current landscape in the music industry and are eager to innovate within it.
You have proven experience working across the multitude of digital platforms available.
Proven track record delivering results and KPI’s through both organic and paid campaigns.
Organized self-starter with the capacity to manage an often intense workload and work in a high-pressure environment.
Values working collaboratively but knows how to work independently to great effect.
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.
Ambition to continuously improve and approach each campaign with enthusiasm and innovative/creative mind-set.

Title: Recorded Metadata Manager (UK)
Description: Your Mission at BMG.We are looking for a Recorded Metadata Manager to join our Supply Chain team and be responsible for the creation and management of metadata for BMG’s UK frontline releases.Our Supply Chain team ensures that BMG recordings reach music fans worldwide both digitally and physically. They are responsible for metadata and asset management for approximately 600,000 recordings and work closely with our manufacturing and distribution partners. Without them, our recorded music releases would never be heard.Your Responsibilities. Working alongside the Frontline creative teams to create and manage new release metadata in BMG’s metadata systemEnsuring all metadata is compliant to the required standardsEnsuring collection of all relevant PPI data for registration with collection societiesRegistration of physical product with MCPSResolving data conflicts and inquiries from internal and external partnersDealing with ad-hoc metadata requests from Synch and Licensing teamsYour Profile. Understanding of music metadata standards, including the iTunes Style GuidePrevious experience of working as part of a supply chain team in the music industryAbility to balance the pressure to deliver a release with the need to ensure all contracts and correct data are in place before deliveryStrong attention to detail and accuracyExcellent organisation and prioritisation skillsEnjoys challenging current practices, troubleshooting and finding solutionsBe a team player with a positive can-do attitude; happy to request guidance as and when necessary to ensure operational excellenceAble to build, maintain and develop relationships with a variety of internal and external contactsProficient with Microsoft OfficeNow, what’s in it for you?25 days annual leave plus 3 days between Christmas & New YearSubsidised gym membershipPrivate Health InsuranceCompetitive pension schemeAnnual gig allowanceArtist showcasesCycle to work schemeSeason Ticket LoanAccess to our Employee Assistance ProgrammeExcellent development opportunitiesA fun and sociable office environment

Working alongside the Frontline creative teams to create and manage new release metadata in BMG’s metadata system
Ensuring all metadata is compliant to the required standards
Ensuring collection of all relevant PPI data for registration with collection societies
Registration of physical product with MCPS
Resolving data conflicts and inquiries from internal and external partners
Dealing with ad-hoc metadata requests from Synch and Licensing teams
Understanding of music metadata standards, including the iTunes Style Guide
Previous experience of working as part of a supply chain team in the music industry
Ability to balance the pressure to deliver a release with the need to ensure all contracts and correct data are in place before delivery

Access to our Employee Assistance Programme
A fun and sociable office environment

Title: General Manager Global Administration (US)
Description: CONCORD IS THE INDEPENDENT, WORLDWIDE LEADER IN THE DEVELOPMENT, ACQUISITION, AND MANAGEMENT OF RECORDED MUSIC, MUSIC PUBLISHING, AND THEATRICAL PERFORMANCE RIGHTS.With offices in Los Angeles, New York, Nashville, Miami, London, and Berlin, Concord has a diverse and vibrant roster of active recording artists, composers, and songwriters, and a vast and historic catalog of works by some of the most treasured names in music history.The newly created position of General Manager Global Administration will actively manage and provide strategic and technical guidance on all Royalty, Copyright and Licensing administration (excluding Theatricals Licensing) for Concord’s Recorded Music, Music Publishing and Theatricals business units throughout the world.The GM will oversee implementation of state-of-the-art software, systems and best practices in close collaboration with his or her department heads, the Finance department and COO. In addition, the GM will maximize efficiencies, communication and transparency for all Concord stakeholders and staff.Core Responsibilities: The GM will have oversight of Concord’s global Royalty, Copyright and Licensing departments, excluding Theatricals Licensing, including staff currently located in the Nashville, New York, Los Angeles, London and Berlin.The Recorded Music and Music Publishing Royalty departments are responsible for:Obtaining and reviewing source files and statements for loading and matching into royalty systemsCompleting quarterly and six-monthly royalty processing runsTimely payment of royalties to all stakeholdersLiaising with managers and stakeholders on all royalty issues.Providing timely royalty accruals to the relevant accounting department.The Music Publishing Copyright department is responsible for: New deal implementationIngesting new songs and catalogsTitle registrations at MRO’s and the Copyright OfficeMechanical licensing and permissionsConflict resolution and data managementClient interactionDeal interpretation and processingLiaising with MRO’s, managers and stakeholders on all copyright issues.The Licensing Administration department is responsible for:Clearance and licensing of sound recordings and compositions for use in all areas of synchronization, sampling and name and likeness.This includes fielding requests from clients, securing necessary approvals, negotiating fees, drafting licenses, and issuing and collecting invoices.The GM will also be responsible for all issues relating to incoming royalty audit and analysis, in close collaboration with his or her department heads, the Finance department and COO.Outline of Specific Functions: Ensure royalty processing procedures are carried out for all designated global territories and divisions in accordance with controls.Prioritize workload for the various royalty department heads adhering to agreed deadlines and royalty timetables.Approval of final run closes for designated Royalty teams and territories.Approval of regular, timely royalty accrual information that is provided to the relevant accounting department.Provide high-level support and training for all global territories and royalty teams.Prepare statistics and detailed royalty reporting for the territories and senior management.Perform analysis of royalty data and processes, including client/rate structures, royalty calculation, and financials.Identify and recommend process improvements and assist with their implementation.Lead the design and testing of system development, department tools and upgrades.Maintain and update royalty procedure and system documentation.Provide strategic advice and direction to the Head of Global LicensingAssist in developing licensing department policies and best practicesReviewing monthly licensing and collection activities and recommend actionProvide advice and leadership to the Royalty, Copyright and Licensing department headsAssist in developing copyright department policies and best practicesAd hoc analyses and projects, as assignedRequirements:10+ years of music industry experience in royalties, copyright, licensing and/or administrationStaff management experienceStrategic planning experience with an ability to communicate effectively at senior management levelExperience of taking a senior role within new system implementation or development

Core Responsibilities:: The GM will have oversight of Concord’s global Royalty, Copyright and Licensing departments, excluding Theatricals Licensing, including staff currently located in the Nashville, New York, Los Angeles, London and Berlin.
The Recorded Music and Music Publishing: Obtaining and reviewing source files and statements for loading and matching into royalty systems
Completing quarterly and six-monthly royalty processing runs
Timely payment of royalties to all stakeholders
Liaising with managers and stakeholders on all royalty issues.
Providing timely royalty accruals to the relevant accounting department.
The Music Publishing Copyright departmen: Title registrations at MRO’s and the Copyright Office
Liaising with MRO’s, managers and stakeholders on all copyright issues.

This includes fielding requests from clients, securing necessary approvals, negotiating fees, drafting licenses, and issuing and collecting invoices.
Outline of Specific Functions:: Ensure royalty processing procedures are carried out for all designated global territories and divisions in accordance with controls.
Prioritize workload for the various royalty department heads adhering to agreed deadlines and royalty timetables.
Approval of final run closes for designated Royalty teams and territories.
Approval of regular, timely royalty accrual information that is provided to the relevant accounting department.
Provide high-level support and training for all global territories and royalty teams.
Prepare statistics and detailed royalty reporting for the territories and senior management.
Perform analysis of royalty data and processes, including client/rate structures, royalty calculation, and financials.
Identify and recommend process improvements and assist with their implementation.
Lead the design and testing of system development, department tools and upgrades.
Maintain and update royalty procedure and system documentation.
Provide strategic advice and direction to the Head of Global Licensing

Staff management experience

Experience of taking a senior role within new system implementation or development

Title: Software Engineer, Frontend (US)
Description: About UsBeatport is responsible for the digital accessibility of electronic music and its associated culture. We have built the largest electronic music store in the world; standing atop a colossal catalogue of inventory, containing millions of tracks catering to the tastes of over a million weekly users from around the globe. And that is just the beginning.We are looking to a hire driven and talented Frontend Engineer to help us transform the platform into the Techno culture hub of tomorrow. Ideal candidates have a strong background in software development, creative & design problem solving, RESTful web services, web audio.This is a high impact role on a small, agile team in Denver, CO.This role is located in Denver, Colorado and requires eligibility to work in the United States without need for visa sponsorship. Applicants who are outside the Denver Metro area and ready to relocate are welcome to apply, but local candidates are preferred.You will:Design, develop, and maintain complex, high performance, and adaptive web applications using HTML/CSS/JSDevelop quality code through unit and functional testingResearch and suggest new technologies to improve product developmentRapidly prototype solutions to validate and prove assumptionsUse your understanding of algorithms and data structures to develop performant solutionsYou are:Proficient in JavaScript developmentExperienced with Node, and front end build toolsExperienced with view frameworks such as ReactExperienced working with JavaScript preprocessors / supersetsProficient with CSS and CSS preprocessing tools such as SassFamiliar with RESTful APIsA purpose driven, problem solver with tremendous thirst for learningComfortable in a Unix/Linux environmentPassionate about electronic music and cultureIf this sounds like you, we would love to chat.BenefitsWe offer a competitive compensation package, plenty of vacation days, VIP access to select music events, and monthly beatport.com credits to keep your library fresh. We hire people who love what they do, and we make sure they have plenty of room for growth. You will work with a team that values collaboration and mentorship. We are always helping one another and sharing empirical knowledge about our stack.Equal Employment OpportunityBeatport strongly supports equal employment opportunity for all applicants regardless of race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, age, marital status, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, sexual orientation, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by state or federal law.Hiring PracticesThe preceding job description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this classification. Beatport may pursue all avenues available, including promotion from within, employee referrals, outside advertising, employment agencies, Internet recruiting, job fairs, college recruiting and search firms.

Title: Project Manager, Regional Mexican (MX)
Description: Project Manager – Regional MexicanSomos ONErpm, una compañía de soluciones para artistas, músicos y creadores de contenido en distintas etapas de su carrera profesional. Casa de 100,000+ artists, sellos y creadores en todo el mundo, ONErpm es una fuerza que está re-definiendo la nueva economía musical, y una de las compañías musicales de más rápido crecimiento en el mercado global.El equipo de marketing está buscando a un nuevo miembro que se encargará de la planeación, creación y ejecución de planes de marketing para artistas y disqueras de regional mexicano. Buscamos a alguien con conocimientos generales y contactos dentro de la industria musical, así como la habilidad de manejar varios proyectos de manera simultánea.En este puesto te encargarás de:La relación con labels y artistas parte de ONErpm.Crear, ejecutar y monitorear planes estratégicos de marketing enfocadas a la promoción de un sencillo o álbum.Explorar nuevas herramientas de marketing.Ser el punto de comunicación entre el departamento de marketing y A&R.Entregar análisis y resultados.La persona que buscamos cuenta con:Al menos tres años de experiencia trabajando con artistas, disqueras y creadores de contenido.Experiencia desarrollando planes de marketing.Experiencia previa en la escena del regional mexicano, así como contactos en la industria de la música y entretenimiento.Conocimiento del entorno digital de la industria, plataformas digitales y estrategias de posicionamiento.Habilidades de comunicación verbales y escritas, que faciliten la relación con clientes y socios comerciales.Habilidad de trabajar bajo presión con diferentes proyectos de manera simultánea.Dominio del idioma inglés.Pasión por la música y la industria del entretenimiento.Vacante de tiempo completo en la Ciudad de México. Enviar currículum a poncho@onerpm.com.We are ONErpm, a solutions company for artists, musicians and content creators at any career stage. Home to 100,000+ artists, music labels, and video creators worldwide, ONErpm is a redefining force in the new music economy and one of today’s fastest growing music companies in the global marketplace.The marketing department is looking for a new member that will be responsible for planning, creating and executing marketing plans for Regional Mexican artists and labels.We are seeking someone who has previous experience in the music business, is extremely organized, and has the ability to coordinate multiple projects at the same time.The ideal candidate will have connections within the music industry and regional mexican scene, and will have the right skills to nurture and strengthen relationships with the artists and their teams.At this position you willCoordinate and strengthen the relationship with the artists and labels of the regional mexican cluster.Create, execute and oversee strategic marketing plans focused on promoting a release.Explore new marketing tools.Be the bridge of communication between marketing and A&R departments.Create and deliver analysis reports and case studies.Our ideal candidate has:3+ years of experience working with artists, labels and content creators.Previous experience in the regional Mexican scene, and connections within the music industry.Previous experience in creating marketing plans.Knowledge of the online landscape, digital streaming platforms and promotion strategies.Excellent verbal and written communication skills that will strengthen the relationship with clients and partners.The ability to work under pressure with different projects at the same time.English proficiency.Passion about a career in the music business.This is a full-time position in Mexico City. To apply, send your resume to poncho@onerpm.comDeadline: February 23rd, 2020.

En este puesto te encargar\u00E1s de:: La relación con labels y artistas parte de ONErpm.
Crear, ejecutar y monitorear planes estratégicos de marketing enfocadas a la promoción de un sencillo o álbum.
Ser el punto de comunicación entre el departamento de marketing y A&R.
La persona que buscamos cuenta con:: Al menos tres años de experiencia trabajando con artistas, disqueras y creadores de contenido.
Experiencia previa en la escena del regional mexicano, así como contactos en la industria de la música y entretenimiento.
Conocimiento del entorno digital de la industria, plataformas digitales y estrategias de posicionamiento.
Habilidades de comunicación verbales y escritas, que faciliten la relación con clientes y socios comerciales.
Habilidad de trabajar bajo presión con diferentes proyectos de manera simultánea.
Pasión por la música y la industria del entretenimiento.

Create, execute and oversee strategic marketing plans focused on promoting a release.
Be the bridge of communication between marketing and A&R departments.
Create and deliver analysis reports and case studies.
Our ideal candidate has:: 3+ years of experience working with artists, labels and content creators.
Previous experience in the regional Mexican scene, and connections within the music industry.
Previous experience in creating marketing plans.
Knowledge of the online landscape, digital streaming platforms and promotion strategies.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills that will strengthen the relationship with clients and partners.
The ability to work under pressure with different projects at the same time.
Passion about a career in the music business.

a passion for storytelling in the audio space; and knowledge of the podcast spaceEstablished relationships with agencies, management companies, writers and music executivesHighly passionate individual, whoâs resourceful and actively seek out unique IP opportunities, develop and execute material at the highest level within budgetStrong communication skills and ability to analyze problems and identify logical solutionsExperience working with music industry talent, managing budgets and production during developmentYou are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or whatâs playing in your headphones. Title: A&R/Label Manager (UK)

Title: Senior Product Manager (US)
Description: Ninja Tune – Senior Product ManagerJOB DESCRIPTION: Work with all label departments & client partners, including agents & managers, to effectively plan & execute marketing campaigns with special focus on our key Counter Records artists.Take a key leadership role in coordinating and energizing various departments to proudly deliver clear, innovative & organized marketing campaigns.Develop robust creative marketing strategies – from ideation to implementation – ensuring campaign goals are exceeded across primary KPIs & sales.Oversee the bigger picture ensuring all areas of a campaign are working together.Coordinate and facilitate set up of digital & physical releases, as well as ensuring logistical processes run smoothly- including but not limited to physical manufacturing, distribution and stock levels, as well as delivery & pitching to DSP partners.Hire marketing retainers and other third parties for press and radio where necessary, drive and manage those relationships and campaigns to ensure effective work.Work with the overall team to ensure campaign assets are procured in a timely fashion, ranging from artwork, remixes, visual content, and social messaging plans.Create and maintain campaign timelines, briefing teams and sharing release information efficiently worldwide.Create detailed reports of all marketing work on individual campaigns, to ensure that all results are communicated to internal staff, global distribution partners, digital sales team, external marketing agencies and artist management.Create and maintain campaign budgets.Collaborate with the digital strategy team to develop integrated digital campaigns and embrace new technologies, social media trends and their benefits.Work with the sales teams to coordinate and confirm a strong physical retail and DSP plan to drive consumption including streams, pre-orders, pre-saves and sales.Coordinate the manufacture of supporting marketing assets including retail posters, stickers, merch, etc.Plan & implement advertising campaigns with the digital team as well as Head of Marketing, including ongoing analysis of effectiveness and insights.Analyze data including social media and sales data to best plan and manage campaigns.Work alongside the entire team to suggest & implement creative marketing to support your campaigns, drive brainstorming sessions and promote collaboration to inspire fresh campaign ideas.Look for new, innovative ways to market and promote our artists, releases, and label especially Counter Records.Manage promotional and launch events where required.Accompany artists, as well as organize, their promo and events where required.All other work as necessary to reach campaign goals.QUALIFICATIONS:Minimum of 5 years of product management experience in the music industry.Minimum of 5 years of experience focused specifically on the US music market with a deep understanding of landscape and key players.Bachelor’s degree, or higher level of education, preferred.Passionate about music, specifically an awareness & appreciation of Ninja Tune.An excellent understanding of marketing and a proven track record managing music industry specific campaigns.Excellent organizational skills.Ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.Meticulous attention to detail.Proven capability to work autonomously, demonstrating the ability to make decisions.Efficient, with excellent time management skills and the ability to prioritize.Drive and ability to push projects through to completion.Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.Ability to work with people effectively and politely at all levels.Excellent relationship-building and relationship-management skills.Ability to handle difficult situations with tact and diplomacy.Excellent ability to problem-solve and keep calm under pressure.Current awareness & understanding of social media platforms & trends.IT savvy with understanding of GSuite (Google Drive, Google Sheets,Google Docs).Hard working and enthusiastic.ABOUT US:Ninja Tune is an independent record label based in London, England, created in 1990 by the duo known as Coldcut: pioneers of the emerging hip- hop/electro scene in the eighties.It has satellite offices in Los Angeles, Berlin and Paris. Ninja Tune and Counter Records have since released music from a myriad of artists such as Bonobo, ODESZA, Maribou State, Big Wild, Kasbo, What So Not and many more.

JOB DESCRIPTION:: Work with all label departments & client partners, including agents & managers, to effectively plan & execute marketing campaigns with special focus on our key Counter Records artists.
Take a key leadership role in coordinating and energizing various departments to proudly deliver clear, innovative & organized marketing campaigns.
Develop robust creative marketing strategies – from ideation to implementation – ensuring campaign goals are exceeded across primary KPIs & sales.
Oversee the bigger picture ensuring all areas of a campaign are working together.

Coordinate and facilitate set up of digital & physical releases, as well as ensuring logistical processes run smoothly- including but not limited to physical manufacturing, distribution and stock levels, as well as delivery & pitching to DSP partners.
Hire marketing retainers and other third parties for press and radio where necessary, drive and manage those relationships and campaigns to ensure effective work.
Work with the overall team to ensure campaign assets are procured in a timely fashion, ranging from artwork, remixes, visual content, and social messaging plans.
Create and maintain campaign timelines, briefing teams and sharing release information efficiently worldwide.

Create detailed reports of all marketing work on individual campaigns, to ensure that all results are communicated to internal staff, global distribution partners, digital sales team, external marketing agencies and artist management.
Create and maintain campaign budgets.
Collaborate with the digital strategy team to develop integrated digital campaigns and embrace new technologies, social media trends and their benefits.
Work with the sales teams to coordinate and confirm a strong physical retail and DSP plan to drive consumption including streams, pre-orders, pre-saves and sales.
Coordinate the manufacture of supporting marketing assets including retail posters, stickers, merch, etc.
Plan & implement advertising campaigns with the digital team as well as Head of Marketing, including ongoing analysis of effectiveness and insights.
Analyze data including social media and sales data to best plan and manage campaigns.
Work alongside the entire team to suggest & implement creative marketing to support your campaigns, drive brainstorming sessions and promote collaboration to inspire fresh campaign ideas.
Look for new, innovative ways to market and promote our artists, releases, and label especially Counter Records.
Manage promotional and launch events where required.
Accompany artists, as well as organize, their promo and events where required.
All other work as necessary to reach campaign goals.
QUALIFICATIONS:: Minimum of 5 years of product management experience in the music industry.
Minimum of 5 years of experience focused specifically on the US music market with a deep understanding of landscape and key players.
Bachelor’s degree, or higher level of education, preferred.
Passionate about music, specifically an awareness & appreciation of Ninja Tune.
An excellent understanding of marketing and a proven track record managing music industry specific campaigns.
Excellent organizational skills.
Ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.
Meticulous attention to detail.
Proven capability to work autonomously, demonstrating the ability to make decisions.
Efficient, with excellent time management skills and the ability to prioritize.
Drive and ability to push projects through to completion.
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.
Ability to work with people effectively and politely at all levels.
Excellent relationship-building and relationship-management skills.
Ability to handle difficult situations with tact and diplomacy.
Excellent ability to problem-solve and keep calm under pressure.
Current awareness & understanding of social media platforms & trends.
IT savvy with understanding of GSuite (Google Drive, Google Sheets,
Google Docs).
Hard working and enthusiastic.ABOUT US:

Title: Head Of Creative And Sales (UK)
Description: At Cezame, we believe that production music is a creative field in its own right.The composers working with us readily embrace this idea as a starting point in the creative process which is ultimately aimed at professionals in the audio-visual sphere.Whether you are a director, editor, producer or sound-scapist, our goal is to share with you our belief and the authentic musical integrity of our composers through their sensitivity, originality and diversity.We are currently looking for a Head of Creative and Sales who will share our vision with TV companies and Advertising agencies, in fact anyone looking for high quality music for their projects in the UK.ABOUT THE JOB:We are looking for someone to really push and sell the Cezame catalogue in the UK.This is a full-time position.You will be responsible for your own time management, but you will be expected to be available between 9am and 6pm.We will give you training in Paris to learn about the company ethos, absorb the catalogue, use the search engine and create playlists.We are looking for someone to travel extensively throughout the UK, promoting the catalogue at conferences and meeting production companies and ad agencies. Travel expenses will be paid for.We would like you to come to Paris about once a month, to meet with your colleagues and to give us a report on the UK market, new strategies and sales.You will receive a basic salary plus a bonus for completed sales.EXPERIENCE REQUIRED:Self-starter with at least 3 yrs+ working in the UK Production Music Market.You love music and can talk about the construction of music and songs.You love meeting people face to face.You love to travel (within the UK and occasionally to Paris, France).You have a proven track record of sales.You have experience in the creative elements of pitching and placing music in media.You have experience in negotiating, pricing and concluding deals from one off placements to blanket licences.You love building relationships and have an existing black book of contacts within media such as music supervisors, ad agencies, brands, marketing and creative executives, TV and Radio producers, editors, producers and talent such as artists and composers.You are well organised and disciplined enough to work autonomously.You can create pitch reports and updates on the local market.You can absorb the music of a new catalogue very quickly.You are familiar with search engines and can create accurate playlists.You are not complacent and will advise your manager of new strategies.You are not a clock watcher and will be flexible as the job demands.You understand copyright and publishing.If you speak French, that would help, but it’s not a deal breaker.WHAT TO DO NEXT?Send in your CV with a short covering letter explaining why we should pick you.Let us know of your approximate salary expectation.If you have the potential, we will then contact you to arrange for an interview.The start date will be dependent on your current situation.WHERE TO APPLY? Please send your CV and covering letter to Jay Mistry jay@jmconsultancy.biz

ABOUT THE JOB:: We are looking for someone to really push and sell the Cezame catalogue in the UK.
You will be responsible for your own time management, but you will be expected to be available between 9am and 6pm.
We will give you training in Paris to learn about the company ethos, absorb the catalogue, use the search engine and create playlists.
We are looking for someone to travel extensively throughout the UK, promoting the catalogue at conferences and meeting production companies and ad agencies. Travel expenses will be paid for.
We would like you to come to Paris about once a month, to meet with your colleagues and to give us a report on the UK market, new strategies and sales.
You will receive a basic salary plus a bonus for completed sales.
EXPERIENCE REQUIRED:: Self-starter with at least 3 yrs+ working in the UK Production Music Market.
You love music and can talk about the construction of music and songs.
You love meeting people face to face.
You love to travel (within the UK and occasionally to Paris, France).
You have a proven track record of sales.
You have experience in the creative elements of pitching and placing music in media.
You have experience in negotiating, pricing and concluding deals from one off placements to blanket licences.

You love building relationships and have an existing black book of contacts within media such as music supervisors, ad agencies, brands, marketing and creative executives, TV and Radio producers, editors, producers and talent such as artists and composers.
You are well organised and disciplined enough to work autonomously.
You can create pitch reports and updates on the local market.
You can absorb the music of a new catalogue very quickly.
You are familiar with search engines and can create accurate playlists.
You are not complacent and will advise your manager of new strategies.
You are not a clock watcher and will be flexible as the job demands.
If you speak French, that would help, but it’s not a deal breaker.
WHAT TO DO NEXT?: Send in your CV with a short covering letter explaining why we should pick you.
Let us know of your approximate salary expectation.
If you have the potential, we will then contact you to arrange for an interview.
The start date will be dependent on your current situation.
WHERE TO APPLY?: Please send your CV and covering letter to Jay Mistry jay@jmconsultancy.biz

Title: Metadata Coordinator (UK)
Description: We’re looking for a Metadata Coordinator to join the team based at our offices in Wandsworth, London.You will be the specialist metadata resource within the group, bringing an obsessive attention to detail and accuracy and at least 2 years experience working in a fast paced, deadline driven digital music supply chain environment.RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE:Working with our labels to source, enter, maintain and validate metadata for all types of releases and products as needed into the Groups master metadata systemsCreating releases, products, recordings as needed by our labels.Work with Digital Production Managers to make sure data available is up to required spec, complete and approved in a timely manner to achieve desired release datesWork alongside the wider team to review data processes and help to write and communicate data best practice process guides and support rolling these out to the businessSKILLS AND EXPERIENCE: At least 2 years in digital music supply chain role, label, aggregator / distributor or DSPGood working knowledge of meta data and its use in respect of digital music supply chain though to DSP’s, MLC’s and widerKnowledge of Content ID supply and best practice.Understanding of digital audio files and digital video requirements in a production environment.Excellent excel skills and total comfort working with data in general.Understanding of XML to assist any troubleshooting via XML Spy or similarSQL skills desirable.To apply please send a CV and covering letter, detailing your salary expectation and your current notice period to jobs@beggars.com

RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE:: Working with our labels to source, enter, maintain and validate metadata for all types of releases and products as needed into the Groups master metadata systems
Creating releases, products, recordings as needed by our labels.
Work with Digital Production Managers to make sure data available is up to required spec, complete and approved in a timely manner to achieve desired release dates
Work alongside the wider team to review data processes and help to write and communicate data best practice process guides and support rolling these out to the business
SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE:: At least 2 years in digital music supply chain role, label, aggregator / distributor or DSP
Good working knowledge of meta data and its use in respect of digital music supply chain though to DSP’s, MLC’s and wider
Knowledge of Content ID supply and best practice.
Understanding of digital audio files and digital video requirements in a production environment.
Excellent excel skills and total comfort working with data in general.
Understanding of XML to assist any troubleshooting via XML Spy or similar

Title: Executive Licensing Officer (FR)
Description: POURQUOI VENIR BOSSER CHEZ NOUS ?Avant tout, un projet fun et hors-du-commun : faire chanter le monde !Au quotidien, ça veut dire: ambiance de travail détendue, bonne humeur et bon humour (on va même jusqu’à pousser la chansonnette régulièrement). Une grande autonomie sur des projets exigeants qui sauront te challenger, des échanges enrichissants avec toute l’équipe, des bureaux en plein centre de Lille (sur 1000m2 avec terrasse et vue sur la Treille), des tournois de Mario Kart / Belote / Coinche / Babyfoot le midi, des cours de Pilates, des personnalités uniques et attachantes !Et pour les perks officiels : un PEE à 200%, des tickets resto blindés, une mutuelle canon, une prime de cooptation pouvant aller jusqu’à 6 000€, des vacances all-in tous les ans (en 2019, c’était une semaine au ski !). Et on en oublie !MISSIONSTu viens renforcer le pôle licensing à la fois en charge des relations avec les éditeurs et les sociétés de gestion des droits d’auteur ainsi que des licences catalogue dans un contexte international.Ta principale mission sera de participer à l’ensemble du processus contractuel (suivi et application des contrats, acquisition des droits d’auteur, mise à jour base de données) ainsi qu’au suivi des licences catalogue et à l’amélioration des outils en place.QUELQUES CHIFFRES14 ans d’existenceUn catalogue de plus de 50 000 titresDes clients prestigieux comme Ubisoft, Nintendo, Universal Music, Twitch…PROFIL RECHERCHÉQuel que soit ton diplôme et ton âge, tu peux valoriser ton expérience par des résultats concrets que tu seras amené(e) à détailler en entretien.Tu maîtrises parfaitement l’anglais, tu as déjà eu une expérience idéalement dans le secteur musical et dans un contexte international.Tu maîtrises également les outils bureautiques, tu seras amené(e) à travailler notamment sur des bases de données.Tu es force de proposition, l’amélioration continue est ta philosophie, tu chercheras donc naturellement à optimiser les process en place.Tu as des bases solides en droit des contrats et de la propriété intellectuelle.Curieux(se), tu es en veille permanente et tu restes informé(e) des dernières évolutions dans les domaines sur lesquels tu interviens.Énergique, tu t’intéresses au travail des autres équipes et n’hésites pas à donner un coup de main.Tu privilégies le collectif à l’individuel et le changement t’enthousiasme.

POURQUOI VENIR BOSSER CHEZ NOUS ?: Avant tout, un projet fun et hors-du-commun : faire chanter le monde !
MISSIONS: Tu viens renforcer le pôle licensing à la fois en charge des relations avec les éditeurs et les sociétés de gestion des droits d’auteur ainsi que des licences catalogue dans un contexte international.
QUELQUES CHIFFRES: Un catalogue de plus de 50 000 titres
Des clients prestigieux comme Ubisoft, Nintendo, Universal Music, Twitch…
PROFIL RECHERCH\u00C9: Quel que soit ton diplôme et ton âge, tu peux valoriser ton expérience par des résultats concrets que tu seras amené(e) à détailler en entretien.
Tu maîtrises parfaitement l’anglais, tu as déjà eu une expérience idéalement dans le secteur musical et dans un contexte international.
Tu maîtrises également les outils bureautiques, tu seras amené(e) à travailler notamment sur des bases de données.
Tu es force de proposition, l’amélioration continue est ta philosophie, tu chercheras donc naturellement à optimiser les process en place.
Tu as des bases solides en droit des contrats et de la propriété intellectuelle.
Curieux(se), tu es en veille permanente et tu restes informé(e) des dernières évolutions dans les domaines sur lesquels tu interviens.
Énergique, tu t’intéresses au travail des autres équipes et n’hésites pas à donner un coup de main.
Tu privilégies le collectif à l’individuel et le changement t’enthousiasme.

Title: Assistant Accountant (UK)

Title: DACH Regional Sales Manager (DE)
Description: DACH Regional Sales Manager About BMAT BMAT started in 2005 when a group of engineers with a passion for music decided to spin-off from an audio research lab in Barcelona. The company started with an idea – to index all the music in the world – and a mission – to better the music eco-system – and a team of nerds eager to learn how to build a successful business. Today the company has grown to more than a hundred people – spread across Barcelona, Buenos Aires, Lima, São Paulo, London, Paris, Oslo, Tel-Aviv, Johannesburg, Singapore, Tokyo and Seoul – that team up to deliver daily 1 million identifications and 3 billion matches to our partners from all over the music industry.Our Culture We love music. It makes our lives a zillion times better. And we want to give something back to her (yes, to us music is a lady). Make her life easier and her future brighter. We apply our scientific, data-infused brains to tell everyone what music has been played – so that creators get the recognition they deserve. We hear everything everywhere and tell everyone who wants to know. ‘What plays around will come back around’ we say. We believe by helping this happen we can make a positive change in the music ecosystem and enjoy talent for another million years.We are a team of pretty decent people who enjoy working with each other. We’re open-minded and well-traveled, coming from all parts of the world. We like coffee, tea, chai, and mate all the same. We’ve got rappers, producers, cello players, DJs, and songwriters. The passion for music, the excitement for technology and Barcelona as a backdrop has proven to make a good combination. We say there is a BMAT touch. We believe it has to do with being passionate, obsessive, nice, generous, honest, self-motivated, entrepreneurial, intrepid, crazy, extraordinary, creative, agile, human, friendly, smart, witty, talented, naive, optimistic, surprising, sensual, and nerdy, but not very hipster (unfortunately).Sales at BMATSales is a core team at the BMAT compound of regional managers, business vice-presidents, and executive directors. Its dynamic is very open, co-operative and no hierarchical. We understand sales as a discovery process through which, based on our expertise, we ask and listen our customers telling us about their challenges and in return we advise them on how we can help them tackle those through innovation.What You Will Be DoingUnderstand the newest needs/requests and problems faced by the Music and Audiovisual industries to propose relevant services.Provide accurate and regular forecasts on the DACH markets to the business development team.Define product strategy together with Global Sales management.Develop relationships with the industry to generate new business development opportunities for our five verticals – TVAV, Broadcast, Digital, Venues, Charts.Spot and answer tender calls.Take the responsibility for the DACH Sales P&L.All About You10 years of experience in sales with a record of quota over-achievement, preferably in a SaaS environment.The ideal applicant will have direct experience and network in the music and audiovisual industries and a good understanding of international copyrights.Experience managing a sales division within Music publishing/ Production music/ Film & TV/Synch is valued.Superior analytical and communication skills.Fluency in English.Bonus TrackAvailability for 3 months training in our Barcelona HQ.This role will require frequent travels within the region.You Can EnjoyFull-time job.Flexibility on working hours.Flat hierarchy.Full autonomy of your dutiesBecoming part of a diverse and multicultural team based in Barcelona obsessed with bettering the music industry.Diverse perspectives bring about challenges and drive innovation, and innovation is kind of our thing. Our open-minded and flexible culture organically fosters this diversity. We’ve learnt that we thrive on learning from each other, so we don’t just welcome but actively hire a range of open-minded people from all walks of life. Our team is made up of people with different backgrounds, perspectives, orientations, abilities, musical tastes, dress sense and time-zones – but we all have one common goal: Making the music world a better place. With equal opportunities for everyone, BMAT is a pretty nice place to work.

About BMAT: BMAT started in 2005 when a group of engineers with a passion for music decided to spin-off from an audio research lab in Barcelona. The company started with an idea – to index all the music in the world – and a mission – to better the music eco-system – and a team of nerds eager to learn how to build a successful business. Today the company has grown to more than a hundred people &#8211; spread across Barcelona, Buenos Aires, Lima, São Paulo, London, Paris, Oslo, Tel-Aviv, Johannesburg, Singapore, Tokyo and Seoul &#8211; that team up to deliver daily 1 million identifications and 3 billion matches to our partners from all over the music industry.
Our Culture: We love music. It makes our lives a zillion times better. And we want to give something back to her (yes, to us music is a lady). Make her life easier and her future brighter. We apply our scientific, data-infused brains to tell everyone what music has been played – so that creators get the recognition they deserve. We hear everything everywhere and tell everyone who wants to know. ‘What plays around will come back around’ we say. We believe by helping this happen we can make a positive change in the music ecosystem and enjoy talent for another million years.
Sales at BMAT: Sales is a core team at the BMAT compound of regional managers, business vice-presidents, and executive directors. Its dynamic is very open, co-operative and no hierarchical. We understand sales as a discovery process through which, based on our expertise, we ask and listen our customers telling us about their challenges and in return we advise them on how we can help them tackle those through innovation.
What You Will Be Doing: Understand the newest needs/requests and problems faced by the Music and Audiovisual industries to propose relevant services.
Provide accurate and regular forecasts on the DACH markets to the business development team.
Define product strategy together with Global Sales management.
Develop relationships with the industry to generate new business development opportunities for our five verticals – TVAV, Broadcast, Digital, Venues, Charts.
Take the responsibility for the DACH Sales P&L.
All About You: 10 years of experience in sales with a record of quota over-achievement, preferably in a SaaS environment.
The ideal applicant will have direct experience and network in the music and audiovisual industries and a good understanding of international copyrights.
Experience managing a sales division within Music publishing/ Production music/ Film & TV/Synch is valued.
Bonus Track: Availability for 3 months training in our Barcelona HQ.
You Can Enjoy: Becoming part of a diverse and multicultural team based in Barcelona obsessed with bettering the music industry.

Title: Global Artist Manager (AUT)
Description: Red Bull’s Culture Marketing program is a successful pillar of our global marketing mix.With the strong focus on Music and Dance but also Art and Design as well as Film we´re covering many fields and are immersed in selected scenes and cultural environments.To strengthen our Global Music Marketing Team, we´re looking for a Global Artist Manager (m/f/d).In this role you’ll collaborate cross-functionally to support Red Bull’s music entities in the ideation, fulfilment and execution of artist led music projects with Red Bull Media Network music content, Red Bull Records, and Red Bull Music Publishing. Through artist and industry relationships that tie into our programs and projects, you’ll ensure that Red Bull builds brand affinity within the music space while paying into Red Bull’s global strategies.Areas that play to your strengths:All the responsibilities we’ll trust you with:PLANNING & COLLABORATIONAs Global Artist Manager, you’ll enrich Global Culture Marketing business plan through proposal of innovative and feasible artist lead event & content projects. You’ll support countries in the execution of artist relations initiatives through the development and implementation of guidelines and tools.MUSIC & CONTENTIn this role, you’ll collaborate with the Red Bull Studios team to identify unique music creation projects. To identify relevant artist stories, help create opportunities to amplify their reach via Red Bull Media Network Music Content team is part of your responsibilities. Furthermore, you’ll support the facilitation of artist deliverables for assigned projects to ensure optimal impact for the brand.RELATIONSHIPSYou’ll identify and maintain relationships with artists and music industry influencers to drive product trial, amplify our existing culture programs, and create unique marketing initiatives. In addition, you’ll work cross-functionally to develop, implement and track artist lead brand integration. To identify scenes and artists that significantly influence brand image with our target consumers is part of your role. Lastly, you’ll manage day-to-day priority artist relationships including managing the delivery of a diverse range of requests across the regional Red Bull business.EXPERIENCEYour areas of knowledge and expertise that matter most for this role:5+ years of experience in a creative-based role (editorial, record label, agency, management, etc.) working directly with music artists on deadline-driven projects3+ years of experience working within a complex brand or advertising agency structureClear understanding of global entertainment, music and culture industry, including legal and licensing, as well as a firm understanding of youth Culture market & scenes globallyAn established network of diverse and well-respected artist and music industry contactsA proven “taste” record identifying, supporting and delivering results for innovative music artists and projectsManagement of collaborative projects from ideation through planning and execution including complex budget managementA strong ability to write, present, and articulate ideas clearlyA history of coaching, leading, and influencing development in order to foster a collaborative and creative environmentBachelor’s degree (eg in Marketing, Communications, Music) or relevant working experienceFluent in English, any additional language a plus

Areas that play to your strengths:: All the responsibilities we&#8217;ll trust you with:
PLANNING \u0026 COLLABORATION: As Global Artist Manager, you&#8217;ll enrich Global Culture Marketing business plan through proposal of innovative and feasible artist lead event &amp; content projects. You&#8217;ll support countries in the execution of artist relations initiatives through the development and implementation of guidelines and tools.
MUSIC \u0026 CONTENT: In this role, you&#8217;ll collaborate with the Red Bull Studios team to identify unique music creation projects. To identify relevant artist stories, help create opportunities to amplify their reach via Red Bull Media Network Music Content team is part of your responsibilities. Furthermore, you&#8217;ll support the facilitation of artist deliverables for assigned projects to ensure optimal impact for the brand.
RELATIONSHIPS: You&#8217;ll identify and maintain relationships with artists and music industry influencers to drive product trial, amplify our existing culture programs, and create unique marketing initiatives. In addition, you&#8217;ll work cross-functionally to develop, implement and track artist lead brand integration. To identify scenes and artists that significantly influence brand image with our target consumers is part of your role. Lastly, you&#8217;ll manage day-to-day priority artist relationships including managing the delivery of a diverse range of requests across the regional Red Bull business.
EXPERIENCE: 5+ years of experience in a creative-based role (editorial, record label, agency, management, etc.) working directly with music artists on deadline-driven projects
3+ years of experience working within a complex brand or advertising agency structure
Clear understanding of global entertainment, music and culture industry, including legal and licensing, as well as a firm understanding of youth Culture market & scenes globally
An established network of diverse and well-respected artist and music industry contacts
A proven “taste” record identifying, supporting and delivering results for innovative music artists and projects
Management of collaborative projects from ideation through planning and execution including complex budget management
A strong ability to write, present, and articulate ideas clearly
A history of coaching, leading, and influencing development in order to foster a collaborative and creative environment
Bachelor’s degree (eg in Marketing, Communications, Music) or relevant working experience
Fluent in English, any additional language a plus

The ability to provide clear, concise information, advice, and recommendations is key.Ability to speak effectively across all levels of business, both internally and externallyExcellent knowledge of the digital space, including retail, analytics, and social media with an ability to adapt to a constantly changing landscapeStrong relationships with media and DSP’s including Spotify, Apple, YouTube, Deezer and Amazon a plusAn enthusiastic approach towards music and creative media, ideally with an appreciation for the repertoire Compass representsBenefits:Competitive SalaryHealth, Dental, VisionPaid holidaysPaid vacation daysRelocation allowanceSend resume and cover letter to jobs@compassrecords.com

Description: A&R Coordinator Tone MGMT Ltd – IntroductionThe main responsibility of the A&R Coordinator is to bring in new artists & provide support the artists in all matters relating to music, design and all visual media and to administer and organise all projects related to this area within the companies under the supervision of the directors.Artist RecruitmentBringing in new artists into the Tone MGMT setup liaising exclusively with the company directors. Musical diversity is encouraged providing artists adhere to the general ethics and principles of the company and current roster.This position must scout and research new artists, coordinate detailed weekly A&R research reports, review demos, cover shows, maintain and develop relationships with songwriters and producers, oversee the signing process and carry out other scouting projects.Artist Liaison Post-RecruitmentLiaison with artists and record/publishing companies on all music related matters, such as recordings, remixes, edits, test pressings, mastering etc.Drawing up budgets for projects and controlling and facilitating expenses under supervision of senior staff/directors.Liaison with artists and record/publishing companies on all visual media projects, i.e. promotional videos, artwork, photo sessions, website design etc. This will include research into video directors, photographers, graphic designers and website design and attending video/photo shoots, overseeing budgets logistics of all related project.Generating and checking royalty statements and processing payments of royalties and expenses under supervision of senior staff and directors and dealing with all queries relating to royalties.Keeping accurate records of contracts, royalties, expenses. Keeping record and cataloguing of all digital recording masters, remixes, videos, photos.From time to time you may be required to carry other duties as appropriate.Skills/experiencePassion for rock & metal music with a deep understanding of the current Tone MGMT roster3+ yearsâ experience within the music scene (any role)An existing network in the UKAbility to work across a broad roster of artistsGood understanding of data analysisExperience in managing budgetsCommercial awareness of the market and music industry developmentsExcellent communication skills, time management and attention to detailAn ability to manage multiple projects simultaneouslyA team player with a can-do attitudeCVâs and covering letters can be sent to recruitment@tonemgmt.com

Artist Recruitment: Bringing in new artists into the Tone MGMT setup liaising exclusively with the company directors. Musical diversity is encouraged providing artists adhere to the general ethics and principles of the company and current roster.

Drawing up budgets for projects and controlling and facilitating expenses under supervision of senior staff/directors.

Liaison with artists and record/publishing companies on all visual media projects, i.e. promotional videos, artwork, photo sessions, website design etc. This will include research into video directors, photographers, graphic designers and website design and attending video/photo shoots, overseeing budgets logistics of all related project.
Generating and checking royalty statements and processing payments of royalties and expenses under supervision of senior staff and directors and dealing with all queries relating to royalties.
Keeping accurate records of contracts, royalties, expenses. Keeping record and cataloguing of all digital recording masters, remixes, videos, photos.
From time to time you may be required to carry other duties as appropriate.

3+ yearsâ experience within the music scene (any role)
An existing network in the UK

Commercial awareness of the market and music industry developments
Excellent communication skills, time management and attention to detail
An ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously
A team player with a can-do attitude

Title: A&R (US)
Description: Based at the iconic Capitol Tower in the heart of Hollywood, Capitol Music Group is home to some of the best-known labels in modern music; encompassing the jazz icons of Blue Note, Capitol’s generations of pop stars, and the legendary recording of Motown to name but a few.We produce, distribute and promote some of the most critically acclaimed and commercially successful music in the industry. This role is a fantastic opportunity to join an organization on the cutting edge of the modern music business and, as part of Universal Music Group, a chance to be part of the world’s leading Music Company.How we LEAD: The chance to join the world’s most iconic music company as an A&R does not come up very often!We are currently looking for creative executives with a passion for hip-hop and R&B music to join our A&R team at Capitol Records and oversee the full artist cycle, from scouting to signing to recording to marketing.The successful candidate will work to grow our artist roster and deliver songs and other opportunities for our artists.The ideal A&R candidate will be highly creative but have a strong business sense, communication skills, and follow through.They will be an expert in hip-hop and R&B and have a deep understanding of and relationships with those genres’ key players and marketplace trends. They will have forward thinking ideas on how to select and guide artists to commercial success. These position(s) will be LA-based.How you’ll CREATE:Identify and sign new talent for Capitol RecordsManage the recording process and coordinate with all parties involved such as producers, writers, mixers, etc.Actively source and build relationships with new artists, managers, lawyers, independent labels, tastemakers, and gatekeepers.Assist A&R efforts on existing label artists by sourcing and developing hit tracks, songs, and other ideas from writers and producers.Manage the production/recording costs budget and negotiations with third parties.Support the creation of all music.Bring your VIBE:Unstoppable work ethic.Passion for music discovery and the creative process.Expert understanding of the music marketplace, with specific focus on hip hop and R&B.Strong network of relationships with artists, managers, producers, songwriters, and tastemakers.Demonstrated understanding of the recording process.In touch with current trends and digital media.Excellent communication and relationship building skills.What’s in it For You?Competitive Compensation Package including Salary, Benefits and Generous 401k Savings PlanPaid Time Off – Paid Holidays, “Gift Week”, Summer FridaysStudent Loan Repayment AssistanceEmployee Developmental SupportAnnual Gym Reimbursement PackagePet Insurance, plus much more!Universal Music Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Title: Junior Programme Manager (UK)
Description: Abbey Road Studios is looking for a highly organised Junior Programme Manager to join the team at Abbey Road Red and help manage their music tech incubation programme and other innovation activities.Follow this link for more details.The A Side: A Day in The LifeAbbey Road Red is Abbey Road’s music tech incubation programme. The first of its kind in Europe and at the forefront of the music technology landscape since launch in 2015, our programme has incubated 15 music tech start-ups spanning the value chain from creation to logistics, marketing and consumption.Our aim is to help brilliant founders introduce the next set of universally adopted technologies into the music business, in the same way our predecessors did.Abbey Road Red also represents our other innovation activities here at Abbey Road. We host yearly hackathons, launch digital music products, explore cutting edge developments in spatial audio and immersive technologies, and produce a programme of knowledge sharing content and events, from Red Talks on cutting edge trends to blogs, video and other social media posts.In the role of Junior Programme Manager you will work closely with Abbey Road Red’s Innovation Manager, helping to run the incubation programme and all its associated activities including scouting, start-up research, diligence, programme management, Red Talk research and production, blog post authoring and more; as well as feed into our other innovation activities from product ideation to helping run Hackathons.The B Side: Skills & ExperienceBe managerial:Programme management: you will be responsible for running the incubation programme with oversight from the Innovation Manager; this will include but not be limited to helping plan incubation frameworks, setting up and overseeing mentorship sessions and workshops with executives from Universal Music and Abbey Road Studios as well as our wider mentor network.Be inquisitive:Start-up research: you will be responsible for helping to scout and research startups; maintaining and updating a large start-up database, producing trends charts and analysis; and handling inbound applications from our online platform and our network.Be administrative:General administration and research: you will be responsible for helping the Innovation Manager prepare presentations and materials for board meetings, internal briefings, conference keynotes and other events; you will be expected to conduct ad hoc research into specific areas of interest as requiredBe collaborative:Marketing: you will work with the Abbey Road marketing team to generate and publish content and marketing assets related to Red’s activity and areas of interest online and on social mediaDigital project management: you will work alongside the Innovation Manager, Head of Digital and Head of Audio Products to manage relationships with developers commissioned to prototype new in-house ideas, test iterative builds and monitor delivery against project plans.Be a confident communicator:Copywriting: you will be expected to generate copy for website updates and blog posts, event outlines, meeting summaries, internal briefings, and other documents.Be organised:Event management: you will be responsible for organising and producing events with oversight from the Innovation Manager; these events will include Red Talks and our yearly Demo Day and start up activations.The ideal candidate will:Be exceptionally organised and efficient and thrive on completing tasks and projects on or ahead of timeExcel in a dynamic work environment with changing day-to-day challenges and tasksBe comfortable producing reports and summaries as well as creative and research-led blog postsBe confident, professional and personable, comfortable meeting new people and building relationships on a regular basis.Continually show initiative and have an analytical mindsetShow a genuine passion for exploring cutting edge emerging technologies.Have a strong understanding of the music technology start-up ecosystem and music business, with a desire to learn moreHave experience using a range of music software and hardware and be familiar with commonly used audio-visual tools and technologies.Be comfortable using Microsoft Outlook, PowerPoint Word and Excel (ideally with good experience in advanced Excel tasks); as well as learning and maintaining any other project management toolsDesirable but non-essential:A passion for music and the meeting points between culture and technology.A knowledge and appreciation of the history of recording technologies.An understanding of content marketing and how to maximise reach and impact on social media.A basic level of experience editing HTML and using WordPress.

Title: VP of Music (US)
Description: Founded and operated in the heart of music city, Soundstripe is one of the fastest growing music technology companies to emerge in the last 5 years.Providing quality music to tens of thousands of video creators – from YouTubers to freelance videographers, nonprofits and Fortune 1000 companies – we believe individual experience and cultural fit are as valuable to our team as resume credentials. We seek the strongest talent in the industry to join us on our mission to Keep Creatives Creating and believe every Soundstripe employee’s alignment with our Core Values is the key to our success.Soundstripe is The Tennessean’s #1 Top Workplace in 2019 for small businesses, and one of the best startup cultures in Nashville. Our Core Values influence every decision our organization makes. We believe these values are what has made Soundstripe special and what has and will continue to drive our growth and success.Provide all customers with genuine and whimsical care.Confront harsh realities with optimism.Keep it light.Strive to always grow and learn.Develop and practice honest communication.Make it better.Date the model. Marry the mission.Be humble and retain a giving and serving heart and mind.Quality over quantity.Done is better than perfect.Position OverviewSoundstripe’s VP of Music will develop, manage, and direct the vision and daily operations of Soundstripe Productions, LLC. Our VP of Music will influence every facet of Soundstripe’s music business, making high-level business and creative decisions concerning Soundstripe’s original content strategy, vision, administration, and operations, as well as decisions regarding the careers of artists on Soundstripe’s roster. The role of Soundstripe’s VP of Music is to strategize, conceptualize and execute our music business’s policy and plans. They will oversee the daily on-goings of Soundstripe Productions business development, staffing, personnel management and collaboration, and all aspects of Soundstripe Productions label operations: production, distribution, marketing, corporate communications, label strategy, and artist discovery and promotion. Soundstripe’s VP of Music will improve the financial performance of Soundstripe Productions, LLC, reporting results and informing Soundstripe’s Board of the original content strategies that will drive Soundstripe Inc.’s growth and success.ResponsibilitiesAs VP of Music at Soundstripe, responsibilities will include but not be limited to:Directing the vision and daily operations of Soundstripe Productions.Strategizing, conceptualizing, and executing Soundstripe’s music policies and plans.Leading with inspired direction, creativity and innovation, collaborating and driving performance through engaging personnel management of Business Affairs, A&R, and Marketing and Sales Directors and teams.Steering the business development of Soundstripe Productions.Overseeing Soundstripe Productions’ business affairs: administration, publishing, and distribution.Overseeing Soundstripe Productions’ A&R: talent and content acquisition strategy as well as music production operations and execution.Overseeing Soundstripe Productions’ sales and marketing: brand direction, artist marketing initiatives and business development opportunitiesReporting on Soundstripe Productions’ financial performance; strategically driving profitability and contributing to the continued success and growth of Soundstripe Inc.QualificationsSoundstripe’s VP of Music will embrace and believe in our Core Values.In addition to embodying these values, our VP of Music will demonstrate the following competencies and skills required for success in this role.Skills & RequirementsExtensive music industry experience, preferably as a senior or executive role within a record label.A deep, proven knowledge of all departments within a record label: sales, marketing, A&R, creative, publishing, distribution, production and promotion.Proven ability to manage vision and purpose, drive results, and lead and develop teams.CompetenciesBusiness AcumenAs Soundstripe’s VP of Music, you know intimately how the music industry works and perhaps more importantly, where it doesn’t work. You will pair your extensive industry experience with your ability to identify and react to creative trends to impart insight and strategy over the daily operations of Soundstripe Productions. You will oversee all of the label’s daily activities, leading with curiosity and competitive insights to ultimately drive the success of its leaders and teams. You will understand deeply what sets Soundstripe’s model apart, and how we stack up against our competition. You’ll always point us toward the direction of our future success.Managing Vision and PurposeAs VP of Soundstripe Productions, you will communicate a compelling and inspired vision and sense of core purpose to our music leaders and teams. You will comfortably talk beyond “today”, about the future and its possibilities. You’ll demonstrate optimism, creating milestones and benchmarks for the creative side of Soundstripe Inc. to rally behind. You’ll cast the creative and business vision for Soundstripe Productions, making the vision shareable by everyone. You will inspire and motivate the entirety of Soundstripe Productions.Building Effective TeamsAs the Executive leader over Soundstripe’s music organization, you will demonstrate an ability to effectively blend people into teams. You will identify the talent and experience required to lead Soundstripe’s music teams, and hire to the strengths that will generate results and bring our creative talent together. You will build strong morale and spirit amongst your leaders and teams, sharing wins and successes, fostering open dialogue, and empowering people to finish and be responsible for the work their responsible for. You will be skilled at defining success in terms of the whole team, and in doing so creating a feeling of belonging amongst the music, administrative, and all Soundstripe Productions teams.Dealing With ParadoxAs a creative business leader, you will be forced to act in ways that seem contradictory. Driving results for a creative organization will require you to be flexible and adaptable when facing tough calls. Communicating the “black and white” of business operations within a creative, “grey” organization will require you to be compassionately tough. You will be counted on to drive the business goals of Soundstripe Productions, while inspiring and uplifting the creative output of our artistic staff. You’ll set strong but flexible standards for the music side of Soundstripe’s business. You’ll be counted on to act in a varying and appropriate manner, based upon the situation, and will be seen as a strong yet sensitive and approachable leader despite the conflicting demands of our artistic business.Strategic AgilityAs VP of Music, you’ll be counted on to “see ahead clearly”. You’ll be relied upon to know our market and the emerging trends that will impact our label’s strategic decisions. You’ll base the plans of Soundstripe Productions’ marketing, A&R, and operations on what will work now and in the future. You’ll stay ahead of the industry curve, pushing Soundstripe Productions to be growing and scaling in a way that is always new and innovative. You’ll push the boundaries of the expected, by tailoring and pivoting our label’s strategies nimbly to serve both Soundstripe Inc.’s mission and bottom line.
