Title: Music Licensing Manager, Advertising and Brands (US)
Description: Our LA team is expanding!We are West One Music Group – a collaborative community of bright minds dedicated to producing music for media for TV, film, advertising, gaming, radio and online. Born in London in 2002, we now have offices around the world as well as studios in several countries, including here in our North American HQ in Los Angeles (Hollywood).We currently have an exciting opportunity to join our team as a Music Licensing Manager, Advertising and Brands. In this role you will be reporting to the VP of Partnerships & Operations, driving new and existing business on the West Coast of the US and Canada. This is a sales role that balances business development and account management.Key Areas of Responsibility Include:Developing meaningful, long-term relationships with an ever-growing list of new and existing clients in the advertising sector, focused on advertising agencies, post-production houses, and direct-to-brand companies.Continually expanding our list of contacts and potential clients who need music to build an active pipeline of sales.Proactively promoting our music to all clients and prospects through face to face meetings, telephone and email communication.Entertaining our clients, giving them what they need in terms of music, and fostering strong creative partnerships.Reporting how clients discover, search, and use music into our systems to enhance the client experience and to keep our database relevant.Practical Skills:You’ll be a natural communicator (in person and in writing), strong relationship builder, and instinctive team player.You will be a true fan of music and how it plays an integral role in the media landscape.You will have a natural curiosity about trends and how to translate in direct client communication.You’ll be experienced and skilled at strategizing outreach activities to help grow the brand presence.You’ll have the capacity to deal calmly with complex and competing demands in a varied sales cycle.You’ll demonstrate an entrepreneurial personality that seeks to connect the people and companies in our industry.  Skills Required:3-5 years’ experience in this sales and account management space, with existing contacts in advertising and brands a bonus.An understanding of CRM/Salesforce and analytical systems and the value of accurate data entry in the sales environment.Proficiency in the full Microsoft Office suite.As a group of people who are passionate about music and people working with music, we believe that finding the right person to join our team is integral to our success.If you are excited about this role, please email Joelle Davis: jobs@westonemusic.com and send your resume with a brief cover letter as to why you’d like to apply.Due to the volume of applications we receive, we are unable to respond to everyone.If you don’t hear back from us within four weeks, please assume that you have not been selected for consideration.

Title: Business Development, Music Content (US)
Description: The people here at Apple don’t just create products — they create the kind of wonder that’s revolutionized entire industries. Title: Music Central Assistant (UK/AUS)
Description: WME, William Morris Entertainment, is hiring for an Assistant within its Music Central team in London and Sydney.WME, William Morris Entertainment, part of the Endeavor network, is a leading entertainment talent agency representing artists and content creators across all media platforms, specifically movies, television, music, theatre, digital and publishing.Headquartered in Beverly Hills, WME also has offices in New York, Nashville, London and Sydney.In 2014 WME acquired IMG, a global leader in sports, fashion and media. All WME employees are required to sign a confidentiality agreementFor London, UK applications, please submit a CV and covering letter to wmeukrecruitment@wmeentertainment.com with the subject line ‘Music Central Assistant London (Music Biz Worldwide)’.For Sydney, Australia applications please submit a CV and covering letter to Jeanine Hamilton jhamilton@WMEAgency.com with the subject line ‘Music Central Assistant Sydney (Music Biz Worldwide)’.

Title: Finance Assistant (UK)
Description: Joining a growing Global Finance Shared Services team and reporting to the Finance Manager, the Finance Assistant will be part of the team responsible for the day-to-day maintenance and control of the global purchase and sales ledger and the timely settling of invoices and expenses. The assistant will also support the finance team with other ad-hoc tasks and projects as required.BACKGROUNDFounded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model. Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We’re currently 600 people based across 10 offices and we’re still growing.WHAT DOES A FINANCE ASSISTANT AT KOBALT DO?Paying Kobalt clients and corporate vendorsLiaising with senior stakeholders throughout the business to ensure timely payment to clientsRecording invoices received and booking these into the accounting systemAgreeing invoices to purchase orders and arranging payments to suppliersMonitoring and maintenance of the accounts payable ledger of the worldwide groupAd-hoc tasks and projects as requiredWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR? Excellent organisational and time management skillsStrong team playerGreat awareness of deadlinesStrong interpersonal skillsStrong Excel skillsImpeccable attention to detailAptitude for working with large volumes of numerical data

Title: Royalty Specialist, Inside Sales (US)
Description: This role will be responsible for providing songwriters, artists, composers, and producers with financing to cover everything from recording sessions and touring, to debt consolidation.The Royalty Specialist will primarily make outbound calls to create leads and build relationships with creatives and referral partners.JOB DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Initiate transactions and build relationships with creatives.Partner with Sound Royalties team to develop solutions tailored to the needs of creatives.Review and prepare pricing models, summary sheets, and other required documents for creatives.Act in an advisory role to creatives regarding finance and royalty matters.Work with creatives to ensure all deliverables are received, reviewed and addressed in a timely manner.Maintain a real-time project/deal pipeline and systematically follow-up on new leads.Track all client-related activities in SalesForce.com CRM.KNOWLEDGE & SKILLS Excellent written and verbal communication skillsProficient in Microsoft Office suite (Outlook, Excel, Word)Strong organizational and planning skillsAbility to remain calm under pressureREQUIRED EXPERIENCE Prior experience working in sales with a focus on outbound calling and relationship-based sales is a plus.Prior experience with music rights and clearances, music publishing, publishing administration, music supervision and/or music copyright production, or other relevant experience a plus WHO WE ARE Sound Royalties is a privately-owned specialty finance firm that helps music industry professionals fund personal and professional projects without ever taking ownership of their copyrights, allowing for pass-through income, and empowering creatives to choose from a variety of flexible pricing options.The company’s core business is offering royalty advances of anywhere from $5,000 to $10 million. It does this by advancing artist, producer and songwriter royalties paid through music labels, distributors, publishers and PROs such as BMI, ASCAP, SESAC, SoundExchange and many more. To date, Sound Royalties has worked with a wide range of leading music industry professionals, including GRAMMY Award winners, platinum recording artists and notable music industry executives in every genre.

JOB DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Initiate transactions and build relationships with creatives.
Partner with Sound Royalties team to develop solutions tailored to the needs of creatives.
Review and prepare pricing models, summary sheets, and other required documents for creatives.
Act in an advisory role to creatives regarding finance and royalty matters.
Work with creatives to ensure all deliverables are received, reviewed and addressed in a timely manner.
Maintain a real-time project/deal pipeline and systematically follow-up on new leads.
Track all client-related activities in SalesForce.com CRM.
KNOWLEDGE \u0026 SKILLS: Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Proficient in Microsoft Office suite (Outlook, Excel, Word)
Ability to remain calm under pressure
REQUIRED EXPERIENCE: Prior experience working in sales with a focus on outbound calling and relationship-based sales is a plus.
Prior experience with music rights and clearances, music publishing, publishing administration, music supervision and/or music copyright production, or other relevant experience a plus
WHO WE ARE: Sound Royalties is a privately-owned specialty finance firm that helps music industry professionals fund personal and professional projects without ever taking ownership of their copyrights, allowing for pass-through income, and empowering creatives to choose from a variety of flexible pricing options.

across media.As part of the team, maximise possibilities for international release through careful co-ordination of plan with international department.Be aware of changes and developments in the music and label services including campaign management, budget management and physical distribution.Excellent knowledge of the digital space, including music retail, analytics and social media, with an skills, with the ability to speak effectively across all levels of business, both internally and externally.Resourceful, accountable, and takes initiative – an expert troubleshooterPassionate about independent music and artist developmentThe Orchard is a pioneering music and video distribution company operating in more than 35 global markets. We provide innovative and comprehensive sales and marketing solutions for content owners working with such diverse artists such as Skepta, The Cribs, Jason Isbell, BTS and Run The Jewels. With an industry-leading technology platform, The Orchard’s creative and tailored approach streamlines its clients’ business complexity while amplifying reach and revenue across hundreds of digital and physical outlets around the world. The Orchard was founded in 1997 to foster independence and creativity in the music industry. For further information, please visit www.theorchard.com.

Title: Senior Global Creative, Creative Solutions (US)
Description: Spotify’s mission is to ‘unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the opportunity to enjoy and be inspired by those creators’.We are looking for a passionate experienced creative to join our Global Creative Solutions team. Creative Solutions is a global group of thoughtful innovators who solve client problems in uniquely Spotify ways. We are strategists, creatives, story tellers, designers, writers, producers, musicians, music fans, data detectives, and innovators. Our mission is to engage, inspire, and guide brands with data fueled creativity.The ideal candidate is a dynamic but thoughtful innovator who solves brand challenges with Spotify creativity. This individual will be responsible for developing proactive creative ideas and concepts that are designed to drive advertising revenue, while working closely with creative strategists to manage key deliverables year round.The role is based in New York but travel to major markets is a possibility when creating culturally relevant creative that is inspirational and actionable for Spotify sales teams to sell in local markets.Collaboration is a key value at Spotify, and this individual must partner closely with various Spotify teams in each market to develop creative solutions that sell. This role, based in New York, reports to the strategy lead for global accounts in NY, USA.What you’ll doBe a champion of Spotify for Creatives, using it to guide the creative community & foster new creative thinking.Develop innovative audio first ideas that forge a new path in what audio creativity isPut our money where your mouth is, by working closely with in house producers to bring your ideas to life on and off the Spotify platformWork closely with product engineers to consider new creative opportunities in the development of new ad products, particularly in digital audioDevelop creative guidelines, best, and “next” practices for creative on Spotify, with a special focus on audio products and innovationBe an inspiring writer that can jump in the studio and record VO to bring your copy to lifeInspire creatives with tips, tricks, and strategies for advertising on Spotify.Work closely with the global strategy team to develop ideas for workshops with top Spotify partners and advertisers.Work closely with global strategists to develop materials for interactive workshopsExpress a consistent, credible editorial voice for Spotify throughout all output; interpret brand partners’ objectives and carve out meaningful places to serve these objectivesWho you areYou have 6-8 years of experience developing and pitching world-class creative concepts within editorial, agency, or branded content fieldsYou have a global approach to creative development, with consideration for content that works across markets, cultures, and audiencesYou are a pro copywriter, comfortable drafting from scratch as well as editing or punching up work from other contributorsYou have a strong portfolio of work that has used audio and/or musicYou are a pro in design tools; eg Photoshop.You are a pro in audio tools; e.g. ProTools.You exhibit curiosity, proactively solve problems, and get things doneYou are able to manage your own time, stakeholders, and accountabilities in a collaborative, fast-paced, entrepreneurial environmentAct as creative expert in internal, client- and industry-facing meetings and eventsYou’re excited by audio, and particularly digital audio’s opportunity for creativity.You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be brilliant. So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the opportunity to enjoy and be inspired by these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service with a community of more than 258 million users.

What you\u2019ll do: Be a champion of Spotify for Creatives, using it to guide the creative community & foster new creative thinking.
Develop innovative audio first ideas that forge a new path in what audio creativity is
Put our money where your mouth is, by working closely with in house producers to bring your ideas to life on and off the Spotify platform
Work closely with product engineers to consider new creative opportunities in the development of new ad products, particularly in digital audio
Develop creative guidelines, best, and “next” practices for creative on Spotify, with a special focus on audio products and innovation
Be an inspiring writer that can jump in the studio and record VO to bring your copy to life
Inspire creatives with tips, tricks, and strategies for advertising on Spotify.
Work closely with the global strategy team to develop ideas for workshops with top Spotify partners and advertisers.
Work closely with global strategists to develop materials for interactive workshops
Express a consistent, credible editorial voice for Spotify throughout all output; interpret brand partners’ objectives and carve out meaningful places to serve these objectives
Who you are: You have 6-8 years of experience developing and pitching world-class creative concepts within editorial, agency, or branded content fields
You have a global approach to creative development, with consideration for content that works across markets, cultures, and audiences
You are a pro copywriter, comfortable drafting from scratch as well as editing or punching up work from other contributors
You have a strong portfolio of work that has used audio and/or music
You are a pro in design tools; eg Photoshop.
You are a pro in audio tools; e.g. ProTools.
You exhibit curiosity, proactively solve problems, and get things done
You are able to manage your own time, stakeholders, and accountabilities in a collaborative, fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment
Act as creative expert in internal, client- and industry-facing meetings and events
You’re excited by audio, and particularly digital audio’s opportunity for creativity.

Title: Global Head of Commercial Development, Music Publishing (US)
Description: The people here at Apple don’t just create products — they create the kind of wonder that’s revolutionized entire industries.It’s the diversity of those people and their ideas that inspires the innovation that runs through everything we do, from amazing technology to industry-leading environmental efforts. Join Apple, and help us leave the world better than we found it.Apple is seeking a senior expert in rights in musical compositions to build and lead a team to manage the commercial relationships relating to rights in musical compositions. This is a key role to support the rapid expansion and growth of Apple Music, iTunes, and all use of musical compositions across the Apple ecosystem. This position is in the new Global Music Publishing group and is based in Los Angeles.Are you a self-starter who is dedicated and demonstrates creative and critical thinking abilities? Do you possess a passion for music, and the craft of songwriting while also having a superior commercial mind? We’re growing and urge you to apply. We believe the role would place you in a highly-energized environment with some of the most talented creative individuals in the industry where you would be inspired to share your years of experience along with providing extraordinary support for the team.Key QualificationsA minimum of 10 years experience working in senior commercial and/or licensing roles within the music industry, ideally within music publishers, collecting societies and/or digital music servicesWe believe an extraordinary understanding of and passion for the global music publishing licensing landscape will be critical to your successYou have an intimate understanding of digital music and the content distribution industryYou possess excellent business development, negotiation, and partnership management skillsYou have a consistent record of working collaboratively within international, cross-functional teams and a high-growth environmentYou are willing to travelExcellent written and verbal communication skills and the ability to build and deliver impactful presentations at all levels of the organizationExcellent organizational skills and attention to detailAn ability to see the big picture and handle a high level of ambiguityConfidence in calling out issues and presenting to senior management on project status and schedulesHoned analytical skills, with an ability to parse through extensive data to extract the key elementsThe ability to build and deliver persuasive arguments in favour of complex concepts.You work well within a fast-paced, time-sensitive environment with proven experience working on multi-faceted, international, large-scale, time-critical projects.DescriptionIn this role, you will build and manage a team that will be responsible for business negotiations with music publishers and collecting societies around the world, building and maintaining relationships with their commercial and business affairs teams to enable the use of musical compositions within Apple as needed. You will work in partnership with the Legal team and the Operations branch of the Music Publishing team on negotiating complex licenses with publishing rights holders to support expansion to new markets, product features, pricing, and commercial programs designed to accelerate the growth of Apple Music. You will also collaborate closely with other elements of the Music Publishing team, as well as other facets of the Apple business, such as the Business Development team to facilitate and build business models for the use of musical compositions throughout the Apple business. A great mind for numbers will stand you in good stead for this role.

Title: Non Executive Chair (UK)
Description: UK Music is an industry-funded body, established in October 2008, to represent the collective interests of the recorded, published and live arms of the British music industry.Through collective representation, UK Music promotes the interests of songwriters, composers, lyricists, musicians, record labels and music publishers (major and independent), managers, producers, promoters, venues and collection societies.We are looking for a new Non Executive Chair to work closely with the CEO to support member engagement and lobbying to ensure the voice of the industry is heard across policy making.Responsibilities include:Leading the UK Music Board, setting the Board agenda and working with the Board and the CEO on strategy, performance and values.Encouraging a culture of openness and debate, managing the Board’s relationship with the UK Music CEO and other executives, to ensure the Board is an effective working group.Ensuring the involvement of all Directors and representatives of Member organisations in UK Music’s work.Promoting effective communication with UK Music’s Members and stakeholders, representing UK Music externally as appropriate.Taking responsibility for the Board’s composition and development, and as Chair of the UK Music Finance and Governance Committee to oversee all governance issues and the relationship with UK Music’s funders.The role has a time commitment of approximately 10 working days per quarter.Our successful candidate will have wide-ranging and relevant experience of the commercial music environment as well as exceptional presentation and communication skills. Candidates should have proven experience in team leadership and conflict resolution and previous experience as a Chair would be a distinct advantage.We actively encourage applications from those who are under-represented in our industry, including women, Black, Asian and Minority Ethnic (BAME) and disabled candidates.To apply in confidence, please send a full CV and covering letter to Rachel Bolland Rachel.Bolland@ukmusic.org of UK Music. by 5pm on Wednesday 15 January 2020.

Responsibilities include:: Leading the UK Music Board, setting the Board agenda and working with the Board and the CEO on strategy, performance and values.
Encouraging a culture of openness and debate, managing the Board’s relationship with the UK Music CEO and other executives, to ensure the Board is an effective working group.
Ensuring the involvement of all Directors and representatives of Member organisations in UK Music’s work.
Promoting effective communication with UK Music’s Members and stakeholders, representing UK Music externally as appropriate.

Taking responsibility for the Board’s composition and development, and as Chair of the UK Music Finance and Governance Committee to oversee all governance issues and the relationship with UK Music’s funders.

Description: Chalk Press Agency seeks a committed, organised and creatively-minded full-time publicist with at least five years of national and online music PR experience.Based in East London, we pride ourselves on a very strong, eclectic and press-friendly roster, and are looking for somebody who has proven experience of breaking new artists and implementing new campaigns to re-introduce established names too.Tenacity and a passion for the artists that we represent is crucial, and the ability to think smartly and creatively rather than simply hammering out emails would be of huge benefit to the successful candidate too.www.chalkpressagency.co.ukIf this sounds like something you’d excel at and you have the necessary requirements listed, please email Jon@chalkpressagency.co.uk with a CV and a covering note that details all your relevant experience, why you’re keen to discuss this opportunity further, and the one press campaign that you’re most proud of to date.

Title: Global SEO Lead (US)
Description: Imagine being a part of an agile team where your ideas have the potential to reach millions.Picture working on cutting-edge consumer-facing products, where every single team member is a critical voice in the decision-making process. Envision being able to leverage the resources of a Fortune-500 company within the atmosphere of a start-up. Welcome to Amazon Music, where ideas are born and come to life as Amazon Music Unlimited, Prime Music, and so much more.Everyone on our team has a meaningful impact on product features, new directions in music streaming, and customer engagement. We are looking for new team members across a variety of job functions including software engineering/development, marketing, design, ops and more. Come join us as we make history by launching exciting new projects in the coming year.Our team is focused on building a personalized, curated, and seamless music experience. We want to help our customers discover up-and-coming artists, while also having access to their favorite established musicians. We build systems that are distributed on a large scale, spanning our music apps, web player, and voice-forward audio engagement on mobile and Amazon Echo devices, powered by Alexa to support our customer base. Amazon Music offerings are available in countries around the world, and our applications support our mission of delivering music to customers in new and exciting ways that enhance their day-to-day lives.Come innovate with the Amazon Music team!We are seeking a talented Global Lead to own Search Engine Optimization (SEO) for Amazon Music, advancing SEO management, growth, testing and optimization globally.In this role, you will identify large-scale opportunities for search engine optimization and translate those opportunities into business requirements, projects and site features. You will run SEO product, content and operations, working closely with dedicated SEO engineering teams.Ultimately, you will be responsible for ensuring that SEO serves as a huge catalyst for our aggressive growth plans. You will be capable of both developing SEO strategies and rolling up your sleeves to implement them.You have an excellent understanding of current best practices, as well as where the industry is headed. You are also a persuasive communicator, evangelizing the value of SEO amongst internal stakeholders.A successful candidate will have strong quantitative skills and enjoy diving deep and discovering the interplay of the many various signals in search engine traffic. The candidate excels at analyzing data, leading initiatives to improve performance, and working across marketing, product and technical teams.Responsibilities:Create a robust search engine optimization strategy for Amazon Music, including a roadmap of initiatives to drive testing and growth.Work closely with technology teams to make sure the company’s infrastructure, architecture, and site features support and enhance the effectiveness of our SEO.Audit and analyze Amazon Music SEO performance, identify issues, find root-causes, and experiment with solutions to improve performance at scale. Communicate SEO performance and initiative status to stakeholders in weekly business reviews.Redefine and evolve key SEO performance metrics, identifying changes and problems as they arise.Lead efforts to solve complex SEO problems, moving quickly to implement ideas and deliver results. Dive into issues and incidents as they occur, perform ad hoc analyses as needed, and/or help escalate them to drive to resolution.Own channel execution globally, contributing to company-wide knowledge sharing and engaging with stakeholders to determine optimal SEO improvements globally.Develop relationships with external third-parties to license technology where it makes sense to buy rather than build.Stay on top of key trends and best practices in search engine algorithms, competitive landscape and the industry to develop winning strategies.NOTE: This role can also be located in Seattle, WA.BASIC QUALIFICATIONSExperience with feature delivery and tradeoffs of a productBachelor’s Degree5+ years of experience in product or program management, product marketing, business development or technologyExperience with end to end product deliveryExperience overseeing roadmap strategy and definitionBachelor’s degree8+ years of experience in digital marketing, product management or related field, 5+ years of experience in SEO at large scale ecommerce or other consumer-facing sites.Proficiency with analytics tools and web traffic reporting including SQL, and ability to do deep data analysis and drive recommendations to senior leadership.Hands on experience with Google, Bing and 3rd party search tools.Proven track record in managing SEO projects from inception to completion.Demonstrated skills in managing complex processes across stakeholders.Passion for metrics and quantitative analysis, and ability to turn analysis into actionable programs.Strong drive for execution and delivering results. PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONSExhibits excellent judgmentHas relentlessly high standardsEntrepreneurial spirit and ability to own/create new programs and processesThinks strategically, but stays on top of tactical executionExpects and requires innovation of her/his teamThinks big and has convictionsHas the innate ability to inspire passion in others

Title: Director of Production (US)
Description: This role will be working closely with our clients, roster of talent, catalogue of music, and executive team to deliver exceptional service on every project from start to finish.Candidates for this role must have previous experience as a creative producer working with musicians, be extremely well-organized and capable of managing multiple projects and priorities simultaneously.For this role you must be both musical and personable, able to easily digest creative direction from clients and translate it to the creative team, anticipate the creative needs of a project and be proactive with the creative talent.Constructive feedback and quality control are critical skills in this role as you are leading the creative direction and team in delivering the best music for each production. Title: Director, Live Entertainment (CA)
Description: GSL Group includes a wide range of businesses including Real Estate Development, Holdings and Construction (Commercial, Industrial, Residential and Sports & Entertainment Facilities), E-Commerce, Sports and Entertainment, Travel & Hospitality, Food & Beverage, and Agriculture.We are looking for a Director, Live Entertainment who is responsible for booking & managing all events in our venues: Save On Foods Memorial Centre (Victoria), Prospera Place (Kelowna) and all our outdoor music festivals (Vancouver, Kelowna, Prince George). The primary focus of this role will be to proactively secure all levels of music/talent for the events and pursue any other opportunities that drive greater community engagement and supportive revenues.In addition, maintaining our current relationships and level of business with our existing promoter clients and core venue tenants is integral to this role.RESPONSIBILITIESThis multi-faceted role will have a number of accountabilities, including but not exclusive to those listed below:Identify potential new events, negotiate agreements; work with the Venue Operations Lead to manage bookings and communicate all schedule information with promoters and agentsWork closely with internal Event Staff, Select Your Tickets team and Promoters on execution of eventsDevelop strong working relationships with key promoters and industry stakeholdersManage date challenges, work closely with WHL and other key tenants to book as many shows as possible to generate greater community engagement and revenue opportunitiesNetwork throughout the industry to negotiate venue rental/booking opportunitiesWork closely with internal and external marketing teams to maximize sales and revenue for each event bookingDevelop key media contacts and manage all event announcementsDevelop ticket information and show manifests for all eventsCoordinate on-sale and ticket settlements with Select Your TicketsEvaluate all risks related to new business and ensure financial and operational precautions are in place; ensure all costing for the event is negotiated as part of venue agreementDevelop all P&L documents for event proposalsDevelop annual operating budget for Live Entertainment; managing cost and ensuring adherence to budgetProvide all relevant information to Finance to facilitate event settlementRepresent venue management on event days with all key stakeholders; Event Manager, Select Your Tickets/Ticketing, Hospitality, Promoter, Tour ManagementManage all Live Entertainment website information to confirm accuracy and timelinessProvide all internal and external communications related to all event detailsSome travel within BC (mostly between Kelowna, Victoria and Vancouver)REQUIREMENTS:Post-Secondary degree in business, sales & marketing or equivalent experience5+ years’ experience in live event production and/or managementExisting relationships within the music and live entertainment industry, ability to develop strong working partnerships with executivesProven experience negotiating new business opportunities and managing long term relationships with key stakeholdersProven experience is developing and delivering digital marketing programs for live eventsAbility to identify and manage the feasibility of new business and revenue opportunitiesKeen attention to detail when it relates to all contract mattersProactive leader with ability to thrive in event-based industry under the pressure of timelines and deliverablesStrong ability to manage a number of projects at one time and make key decisions as neededStrong written and verbal communication skillsSponsorship, ticketing, VIP experience.Role is primarily dayshift, however, must be available evenings and weekends with ability to travel as required, mostly within BC (this will be based on event schedule)THE IDEAL CANDIDATE:Is creative, innovative and has a keen understanding of the music and entertainment industryHas the ability to be innovative and insightful – constantly looking for new ways of doing thingsSomeone who is highly organized and detail-oriented; ability to handle multiple projects with tight timelinesIs a team player who works well with others and is highly collaborative and focuses on building relationshipsIs receptive to new ideas and approaches; adapts well to change; remains positive and calm under pressureHas very strong business acumen, and excellent analytical skills, with ability to assess the potential of ticket salesHas excellent interpersonal and communication skills (written and verbal)Compensation: will be commensurate with experience.GSL is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and are dedicated to providing a work environment that is free from harassment and discrimination. All employment decisions are made based on qualifications, job requirements and business needs. GSL is passionate about being known for an inclusive culture where talented people of all backgrounds want to work.

RESPONSIBILITIES: Identify potential new events, negotiate agreements; work with the Venue Operations Lead to manage bookings and communicate all schedule information with promoters and agents
Work closely with internal Event Staff, Select Your Tickets team and Promoters on execution of events
Develop strong working relationships with key promoters and industry stakeholders
Manage date challenges, work closely with WHL and other key tenants to book as many shows as possible to generate greater community engagement and revenue opportunities
Network throughout the industry to negotiate venue rental/booking opportunities
Work closely with internal and external marketing teams to maximize sales and revenue for each event booking
Develop key media contacts and manage all event announcements
Develop ticket information and show manifests for all events
Coordinate on-sale and ticket settlements with Select Your Tickets
Evaluate all risks related to new business and ensure financial and operational precautions are in place; ensure all costing for the event is negotiated as part of venue agreement
Develop all P&L documents for event proposals
Develop annual operating budget for Live Entertainment; managing cost and ensuring adherence to budget
Provide all relevant information to Finance to facilitate event settlement
Represent venue management on event days with all key stakeholders; Event Manager, Select Your Tickets/Ticketing, Hospitality, Promoter, Tour Management
Manage all Live Entertainment website information to confirm accuracy and timeliness
Provide all internal and external communications related to all event details
Some travel within BC (mostly between Kelowna, Victoria and Vancouver)
REQUIREMENTS:: Post-Secondary degree in business, sales & marketing or equivalent experience
5+ years’ experience in live event production and/or management
Existing relationships within the music and live entertainment industry, ability to develop strong working partnerships with executives
Proven experience negotiating new business opportunities and managing long term relationships with key stakeholders
Proven experience is developing and delivering digital marketing programs for live events
Ability to identify and manage the feasibility of new business and revenue opportunities
Keen attention to detail when it relates to all contract matters
Proactive leader with ability to thrive in event-based industry under the pressure of timelines and deliverables
Strong ability to manage a number of projects at one time and make key decisions as needed
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Role is primarily dayshift, however, must be available evenings and weekends with ability to travel as required, mostly within BC (this will be based on event schedule)
THE IDEAL CANDIDATE:: Is creative, innovative and has a keen understanding of the music and entertainment industry
Has the ability to be innovative and insightful – constantly looking for new ways of doing things
Someone who is highly organized and detail-oriented; ability to handle multiple projects with tight timelines
Is a team player who works well with others and is highly collaborative and focuses on building relationships
Is receptive to new ideas and approaches; adapts well to change; remains positive and calm under pressure

Title: Music Publishing Administrator (UK)
Description: Wixen Music UK Ltd. has a position available for a music publishing administrator at entry level.This position is for a fixed contract of a year, which could become permanent depending on performance and the company’s needs.The applicant must have excellent organisational and time management skills, close attention to detail, and be able to work well with others as part of a small team.The individual must present well in person and on the phone. The successful candidate will have the following attributes: hard working, reliable, responsible, trustworthy and able to work to tight deadlines. Must have in-depth knowledge and experience with Excel and Word.Prior experience in music publishing and neighbouring rights would be advantageous, but we are willing to train and educate the successful candidate, provided they possess the skills listed above. Proficiency in a secondary language would be beneficial, but not essential.Please send a covering letter and CV to info@wixenmusic.co.uk, FAO Tania Woodcock.The deadline for all applications is Thursday 30th of December.

Title: Label Assistant (UK)
Description: Circus Records and its associated group of companies are looking for a new addition to their growing team.You will have both responsibility and involvement within general planning, project setup, social media planning and execution, communication with artist/manager and overall project deliveries.As an assistant within the label team you will be entrusted to support, plan and execute a number of processes on behalf of the record label and wider stakeholders.We are looking for a candidate who has intern level experience in a similar role within the music industry. You should be able to display strong communication skills and a high level of attention to detail is a must.Ideally those that apply will have also worked across social media management and brand support, plus have a grasp of modern record label functions and digital mediums. Understanding of the record industry and/or direct label experience would be viewed as beneficial.As a direct link between the label and our artist/managers you must display confident communication, management and people skills.As a group we are always looking for hard working, self-motivated individuals who can add value to our growing family of businesses.Ideally the perfect candidate will be someone looking to develop within our company and grasp the opportunities that we can offer.If you’re interested in joining this group of forward thinking international entertainment companies, please email your CV and covering letter demonstrating your experience and successful track record to enquiries@circus-records.co.uk with the subject ‘Label Assistant’.The closing date for applications is Wednesday 18th December.

Title: Finance Director (NO)
Description: MER Group er et norsk musikkselskap og artistrepresentant, fordelt i fire datterselskaper.MER sin visjon er å bli bransjens beste på å kvalitetssikre, utvikle, fasilitere og forvalte alle aspekter ved våre klienters karriere og virke. Selskapsgruppen har bred kompetanse og tilbyr tjenester innenfor management, labelfunksjoner, forlagsvirksomhet, musikkproduksjon, merkevarebygging og øvrig innholdsproduksjon. MER har hovedkontor i attraktive lokaler i Oslo sentrum, som huser både kontorlandskap og innspillingsstudioer. Med internasjonalt anerkjente klienter som Alan Walker, K-391, Vinni, Paperboys og Mood Melodies, opererer MER med hele verden som arbeidsplassVil du være med å lede utviklingen av et selskap i sterk vekst?MER Group opplever sterk vekst og søker nå etter sin nye finance director.I denne rollen får du muligheten til å være med å forme og videreutvikle MER Group sin økonomifunksjon, samt være med på å sette retningen for selskapet gjennom gode forretningsanalyser og strategiske beslutninger. Du vil få overordnet ansvar for regnskap og økonomi for MER Group.Du blir en del av et kreativt gründermiljø som har store ambisjoner for fremtiden. Sammen jobber vi med å bygge noen av verdens største artister i et globalt marked. Arbeidsdagene er preget av stort engasjement, et sterkt fagmiljø og godt humør. Hos oss er det takhøyde for å være uenig og ta en diskusjon. Vi jobber sammen for at du skal nå dine mål, og du vil få rom til å lykkes i rollen. Vi er flinke til å feire oss selv med sosiale begivenheter – vårt julebord i år er på Bali!Du blir en del av ledergruppen og involveres i selskapets strategiske, taktiske og operasjonelle planlegging. Du vil lede et team på to dyktige medarbeidere. Sammen med 17 kollegaer vil din arbeidsplass være i moderne lokaler med attraktiv beliggenhet i Oslo sentrum.Hvem er du?Du er nytenkende og ambisiøs på selskapet vegne, har god forretningsforståelse og setter deg raskt inn i nye problemstillinger. Vi ser etter deg som er positiv og fremoverlent med entusiasme. For å lykkes i rollen er det viktig at du evner å tenke helhetlig, og trives med å jobbe både strategisk og operativt. Du kommuniserer godt og skaper tillit, samtidig som du ikke er redd for å utfordre «status quo». Hos oss er det høyt tempo i arbeidshverdagen som krever at du er tilpasningsdyktig og evner å jobbe godt i team og på egenhånd. Som finance director hos oss er det viktig at du er analytisk, strukturert og initiativrik i tilnærmingen til arbeidet.Ansvar- og arbeidsoppgaverVidereutvikle selskapets rapporteringsstruktur og KPI-styringUtvikle gode forretningsanalyser og business case for videre beslutningsgrunnlagPådriver for effektivisering av prosesser på tvers av selskaper og landegrenserStrategisk utvikling av selskapet i samarbeid med ledergruppen og eiereAnsvarlig for prognose, budsjett og likviditetsstyringAnsvarlig for månedlig og kvartalsvis rapportering, samt årsregnskapLede og motivere to dyktige medarbeidereKvalifikasjonerHøyere utdanning fra universitets- /høgskolenivå, på minimum bachelorgradMinimum 3-5 års relevant erfaring, gjerne fra vekstbedrifter/ start-up selskaperErfaring med prosjektregnskapErfaring med håndtering av store datamengder/ komplekst datagrunnlagMeget god skriftlig og muntlig fremstillingsevne på norsk og engelskSvært gode Excel-kunnskaperGod systemforståelseKjennskap til ulik internasjonal forretningskultur og flerkulturell forståelse er fordelaktigLedererfaring er en fordelInteressert?For mer informasjon om stillingen, ta gjerne kontakt med våre rådgivere i Experis Sissel Melkild, 40639890/ sissel.melkild@no.experis.com og Katrine Jayne H. Wiksnes 40639828/ katrine.wiksnes@no.experis.com. Vis interesse ved å sende inn CV og søknad snaret, og senest innen 22.12.2019.

Hvem er du?: Du er nytenkende og ambisiøs på selskapet vegne, har god forretningsforståelse og setter deg raskt inn i nye problemstillinger. Vi ser etter deg som er positiv og fremoverlent med entusiasme. For å lykkes i rollen er det viktig at du evner å tenke helhetlig, og trives med å jobbe både strategisk og operativt. Du kommuniserer godt og skaper tillit, samtidig som du ikke er redd for å utfordre «status quo». Hos oss er det høyt tempo i arbeidshverdagen som krever at du er tilpasningsdyktig og evner å jobbe godt i team og på egenhånd. Title: VP, Creative, Latin (US)
Description: Our client, a global leader in music rights management, are seeking a new Vice President Creative for their Latin market, based in LA, to create and maintain relationships with songwriters, artists and publishers worldwide and within the music community at large.The role will include:Creating and sustaining Industry relationships in the music business to build the clients presence as leader in the Latin music community.Optimizing relationships to deliver greater market share and favorable catalogue assets.Leading a team in establishing and maintaining close working relationships with established writers and publishers, their agents, managers and attorneys.Proactively following current and emerging music trends to facilitate a constant flow of new music to build their market share across all genres and platforms within Latin.Demonstrating an ability to identify budding talent and then a willingness to support, foster and grow talent while connecting and maintaining business relationships.Addressing issues unique within the Latin music community while at the same time aligning to the value, principles and strategies for the company.Working with the Creative leadership team to define innovative songwriter/ composer developmental strategies to attract and develop new writers and composers to ensure diversity and growth in their repertoire.Leading and participating in Creative recognition events ranging from small congratulatory dinners to the large award events.Collaborating with the Corporate Communications team to develop branding and promotional strategies that position the company and its affiliates as a strong creative and business-minded force within the Latin music community.Formulating and monitoring annual budgets for Latin and provide guidance on cost controls to align with organizational strategies.The ideal candidate will possess a University degree in a related field; Master’s degree or equivalent preferred.Qualified candidates should have an extensive experience in an executive position interfacing with creators, users and/or rights owners within the music industry.Must be bi-lingual and able to speak and write in Spanish.An awareness and thorough understanding of the music publishing arena is essential, experience working with a large network of industry professionals and a passion for Latin music and proven extensive connections within this genre essential.Please email your CV with a covering letter to helen@themusicmarket.co.uk

The role will include:: Creating and sustaining Industry relationships in the music business to build the clients presence as leader in the Latin music community.
Optimizing relationships to deliver greater market share and favorable catalogue assets.
Leading a team in establishing and maintaining close working relationships with established writers and publishers, their agents, managers and attorneys.
Proactively following current and emerging music trends to facilitate a constant flow of new music to build their market share across all genres and platforms within Latin.
Demonstrating an ability to identify budding talent and then a willingness to support, foster and grow talent while connecting and maintaining business relationships.
Addressing issues unique within the Latin music community while at the same time aligning to the value, principles and strategies for the company.

Working with the Creative leadership team to define innovative songwriter/ composer developmental strategies to attract and develop new writers and composers to ensure diversity and growth in their repertoire.
Leading and participating in Creative recognition events ranging from small congratulatory dinners to the large award events.

Collaborating with the Corporate Communications team to develop branding and promotional strategies that position the company and its affiliates as a strong creative and business-minded force within the Latin music community.
Formulating and monitoring annual budgets for Latin and provide guidance on cost controls to align with organizational strategies.

Title: Editorial Product and Features Project Manager (US)
Description: The people here at Apple donât just create products â they create the kind of wonder thatâs revolutionized entire industries.Itâs the diversity of those people and their ideas that inspires the innovation that runs through everything we do, from amazing technology to industry-leading environmental efforts. anticipating and resolving problems.You are well versed in pop culture, music and are passionate about entertainment.You have had experience working across many International cultures and countries with both an understanding for local nuancesYou have knowledge of search engine optimization (SEO) strategy and planning.You are results-oriented, upbeat and focused with a strong passion to learn and succeed.Are you flexible and versatile in nature? Title: VP of Creative Sync (US)
Description: This role will be working closely with our roster of talent, catalogue of music, and Executive team to strategize and execute on creative pitches for sync, custom music projects, artist releases, and branding partnerships.Candidates for this job must have previous experience in a creative role, project management, and an extremely well-organized mind capable of managing multiple overlapping projects and opportunities on a daily/weekly/monthly basis.For this role you must be a strong communicator. Title: Member Services Coordinator (UK)
Description: Merlin is looking for an enthusiastic and passionate person to join our London Member Services team as Member Services Coordinator.The position, based in London, will report to Merlin’s Director, Global Member Services and work closely with Merlin’s Member Services Manager (UK) where you will play an important role in all communications with members, our global team and the membership application process.The role will involve working with Record Labels and Distributors from across the globe who are seeking to join Merlin as well as working with existing members and DSPs.Merlin has experienced remarkable growth since its inception and is currently expanding its team.This is an opportunity for someone with excellent people skills to work with a broad cross-section of the global independent recorded music industry, in a supportive working environment that demands technical excellence.The successful candidate will be a motivated and proven self-starter, with a demonstrated ability to work independently and be confident in resolving queries from music labels and distributors on a global basis.Excellent verbal and written communication skills and a strong knowledge of the digital music industry are essential and established relationships and an understanding of the independent music sector would be advantageous.You’ll find the full job description hereHow to applyPlease send resume and cover letter with salary requirements to jobs@merlinnetwork.org.Deadline to submit is 10 December 2019.About Merlin Merlin is the digital rights agency for the world’s independent label sector.The organisation’s members command in excess of 12% of the digital recorded music market and embody more than 20,000 independent record labels and distributors from 63 countries.These include Altafonte Distribution, Armada Music, Beggars Group, Curb Records, Dim Mak, Domino, Eleven Seven Music Group, Entertainment One, Epitaph Records, !K7, Kobalt Music Recordings/AWAL, Mad Decent, Merge Records, mtheory, Mom + Pop, Ninja Tune, [PIAS], Redeye Distribution, Secretly Group, Symphonic Distribution, Sub Pop and Warp Records, representing some of the world’s most important and successful artists.Merlin acts to ensure these companies have effective access to new and emerging revenue streams and that their rights are appropriately valued and protected.Since commencing operations in May 2008, Merlin has licensed over 25 key digital services including Facebook, YouTube Music, Spotify, Deezer, Pandora, Alibaba, NetEase and Tencent, and has reached a number of high-value copyright infringement settlements on behalf of its members.Merlin has won ‘Independent Champion’ at the A2IM Libera Awards three times – in ‘15, ‘16 and ‘18. The company’s CEO, Charles Caldas, has been recognised by Billboard in its 2019 list of the 100 most influential people in the music business, for the second year running, while Merlin has been named as an advocate for the publication’s 2018 ‘Indie Power Players’.Merlin has offices in London, New York and Tokyo.

About Merlin: Merlin is the digital rights agency for the world’s independent label sector.

Manage Backline Cleveland communications and social media platforms.

Experience and existing relationships in the music and entertainment industry is preferred.
Ability to work in a fast-paced, highly independent environment and juggle multiple ongoing projects, responsibilities and external relationships.
Willingness to intensively and rapidly dive deeper into the industry, even if already familiar.
Flexibility to travel and work some nights and weekends– we’re not a Monday-Friday, 9-5 operation!
At least one year of program or product management experience a plus.
Strong interpersonal and relationship-building skills.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
A “no job is beneath me” mentality.
At gener8tor we\u2026: Revolve everything we do around helping our customers–the startup founders, musicians and artists.
Wipe down the tables after lunch with as much enthusiasm as we have about attending a concert.
Are motivated by building a better tomorrow for the communities we work in.
Observe no boundaries around responsibility and work as a team to get $#!^ done!

PC (MS Word, PowerPoint, Excel, Outlook etc); Mac OS (keynote, itunes, etc).Strong knowledge of Social Media; Comfortable reading online analytics.Recommended Sony Systems to be familiar with: SAMIS, NextBigSound, PRS, GLORES, Digital Exchange.Ability to travel internationally frequently.Multicultural sensibility and tolerance.Strong social skills; Title: Artist Management Assistant (UK)
Description: ABOUT USSupernature is an independent artist services company, partnering with exceptional creators across music and visual arts.With a firm focus on artistic freedom, and a range of in-house capabilities including marketing, distribution, content creation, product development and business management, we are uniquely positioned to help independent artists build successful, sustainable careers.Read more about Supernature hereTHE ROLEWe are looking for an organised, proactive Artist Management Assistant to work full-time from our London office in Elephant & Castle. Description: Vydia’s Rights Management Department oversees all digital rights-related functions including, but not limited to, master and publishing rights management, content deployment, partner asset management, internal catalog management, agreement review, copyright claims, and general quality control.We are seeking a Rights Management Coordinator to join our Holmdel, NJ office immediately.This position will report directly to the Director of Rights Management and be responsible for supporting the day-to-day operations of the department.The ideal candidate will have a deep understanding of intellectual property rights and U.S. Copyright law, experience in content management, and a self- starting attitude. Title: Global Podcast Manager, Content Communications (US)
Description: This role will work with Spotify’s Content Communications team to map out, execute and analyze global campaigns supporting numerous podcast projects in every imaginable genre around the world. Beginning with market by market analysis and project announcements, carrying through show launches and then sustaining programming audience growth efforts — this individual will work with Spotify podcast creators, talent and their representatives on a daily basis.For this role, you must be a strong writer and communicator with a high level of comfort with frequent travel. Experience managing multiple overlapping projects and established relationships with top business, entertainment and trade press is also essential.Do you enjoy foreign language films, international TV and global music? Do you have experience assessing an audio show or series as it is being developed? Have you plotted out and prioritized the rhythm of a publicity campaign while sourcing content from multiple departments and stakeholders to deliver verifiable results? Do you enjoy drafting announcements, debating just the right adjective for a positioning statement, as well as obsessively reading every study and trend piece on the podcast space that the internet has to offer? Are you comfortable directly pitching press, researching & outlining Q&A strategies, designing the communications plan for an awards campaign and then relaxing by pouring over show data to identify the next audience that we should pursue? If so, then please keep reading.Success in this role requires a passion for supporting creators, executives and our growing slate. You should be familiar with global Spotify business priorities and how they ladder up to company objectives, as well as how to create understanding and connectivity with the rest of the company. You also need to be familiar with different global market needs in order to help localize best practices. Above all, your work will impact the way that the world understands and learns about Spotify’s ongoing efforts to grow the global audio ecosystem. If you have a love for podcasts, creators and global culture, this might be the job for you. What you’ll doWork with the Content Communications team in markets around the world to plan and optimize podcast publicity campaignsHelp design strategy and work to support global markets with toolkits/messaging/best practicesDraft, contribute to media announcements surrounding new podcasts and trend storiesWork alongside multiple agenciesBuild and maintain strong relationships with key media in order to drive communication objectives and generate accurate and positive coveragePerpetually remain up to speed on the latest market by market news in the entertainment, technology and podcasting spaceBrief and create messaging and supporting research for Spotify’s key spokespeople and talent for announcements, panels and interviewsWork closely with internal data resources to deeply understand consumer behavior and identify opportunities to capitalize on that informationWork in close coordination with our global Communications team from our offices in Los AngelesWho you are 5-7+ years relevant communications experience in entertainment or business public relations, digital marketing, and podcast industry — must have agency, network or studio experienceA deep love for podcasts, entertainment, music and technologyExcellent writing, organizational and presentation skillsProficiency in multiple languages is not a requirement but is a significant plusStrong media relationships and the ability to cultivate additional contacts in the spaceAbility to multitask and work calmly in an extremely fast-moving industry and environmentCollaborative and creative individual who enjoys partnering closely across teams and departments to create and distribute the most impactful communications possibleAbility to travel internationally on a regular basisOf course, a proud representative of the Spotify brand!

Title: Director of New Media, Sync (UK)
Description: With its broad and diverse roster of new stars and legendary artists, Warner Music Group is home to a collection of the best-known record labels in the music industry including Asylum, Atlantic, East West, Elektra, FFRR, Fueled by Ramen, Nonesuch, Parlophone, Rhino, Roadrunner, Sire, Warner Records, Warner Classics and Warner Chappell Music, one of the world’s leading music publishers with a catalogue of more than one million copyrights worldwide.For more than four decades, WMG has been an industry-leading force in providing a world-class array of services designed to help artists and labels grow their careers and their businesses. Artist & Label Services is the umbrella for WEA (Warner-Elektra-Atlantic) – the pioneering WEA distribution and marketing network – and Alternative Distribution Alliance (ADA) – the ground-breaking global distribution company for independent artists and labels.A little bit about our team:We’re a close-knit team of 10 music-lovers, highly encouraging of each other and keen to impress our artists. The essence of our team, within Warner Records, is partnering with a wide range of media companies to influence and secure placements on film content, for our huge roster of music. We work across the entire Warner Records catalogue, from superstars through to breaking-artists. We also own a series of offshoot initiatives to help us grow our business. Such as W songs – a development publisher for brand-new talent. As film content includes anything from the latest blockbuster to random YouTube videos, we have our work cut-out, to understand who, what, when, where and why the next exciting content will come from.Why this could be your next big break:Never has this area of our business been so important. Many of the artists we work with are keen to have their music synced to picture and nearly all care about this outlet for their creative work. We work hard to provide as many opportunities as possible, however as this area becomes more congested we need to become smarter in our approach. This role is at the forefront of our creative work, implementing exciting new ways to celebrate our music. Unlike traditional sync, this role explores creatively how we place our music against picture, whether that be via technology solutions, strategic signings or on new growing channels.Here you’ll get to:Working with the VP of Sync, lead a new sync strategy within the New Media and tech space.Handle the commercial offering of our in-house publishing arm, within Film/TV and Games.Launch new initiatives into the marketplace and make key decisions for their success.Work closely with artists and managers to realise their vision in our space.Explore and build opportunities with external partners for our artists and WMG UK.About you:You are culturally curious and open-minded with a start-up attitude.You love music.You are a standout colleague.You spot trends, follow leads and close down opportunities.You’re a phenomenal communicator, and an even better ambassador.You’re a forward-thinking negotiator.You have 9+ years in the entertainment/tech industries, including experience working at or with music companies.It would be music to our ears if you also had:Experience within sync or a licensing environment.Experience with Keynote and deck creation.

Title: Director Of Marketing, Communications & Public Affairs (UK)
Description: The Ivors Academy of Music Creators is a non-profit membership association that has supported, protected and celebrated music creators in the UK for the last 75 years.It is an exciting time in our history, having rebranded from the BASCA to The Ivors Academy of Music Creators in March this year, we have an ambitious strategy of growth which is seeing us expand into new areas with new partners. This is a pivotal role for a creative, ambitious and skilful communicator and marketer with experience of policy development, who understands the changing music landscape and wants to be at the heart of the debate as a champion of music creators.ABOUT THE ROLEReporting to the CEO you will be responsible for the effective management of messaging across the organisation to drive recruitment of new members, increase positive engagement with existing members and partners, maximise awareness and impact of our campaigning activities with opinion formers.Duties include:Development and implementation of The Ivors Academy Brand strategy,Development and implementation of marketing strategies including events, advertising, digital marketing, PR and partnerships to drive increased membership,Development and implementation of communications strategies for existing members including website, email, social media, print, video, podcasts and direct mail,Day to day management of the new Ivors Academy Policy Unit including policy development, press and public affairs strategy,Maintaining a network of industry partners to maximise awareness and impact,Management of our Equality, Diversity and Inclusion agenda,Development and management of The Ivors Academy Youth Network and Ivors Academy Friends offering which seek to increase the awareness and support for Academy initiatives and recruitment,Day to day management of Academy Genre Committees, marketing, communications, policy and equality team,Management of third-party providers and consultants as required.Essential Skills and Person Specification:Significant experience including qualifications in marketing, communications and PR,Experience working in a membership organisation context,Evidence of setting and implementing successful strategies,Has creatively led a team that deliver engaging and impactful communications,Demonstrates passion and skills in campaigning, lobbying and PR,Thrives on the challenge of managing multiple projects at any one time,Demonstrable experience and knowledge of CRM and segmentation strategies,Experience of managing teams and resources,Excellent written and verbal communication skills,Confident in a public-facing role connecting across the industry,A self-starter who can create opportunities,Knowledge and contacts in the music industry a plusTo apply, please send a CV and an accompanying cover letter to Graham Davies, CEO at: contact@ivorsacademy.comApplication deadline: Monday 25 November 2019Interviews: week commencing 2 December 2019 Start date: As soon as possibleAbout The Ivors AcademyThe Ivors Academy exists to support, protect and celebrate music creators in the UK. We are the independent professional association representing songwriters and composers in all genres, whether they create song, symphony or sync. We are entirely self-funded, relying on the continued support of our members and partners to carry on our work. Music is the most profound, enduring and popular of art forms. It crosses all borders, conveys all emotions, and touches all people. None of the music that the world loves exists until brilliant songwriters and composers create it. We represent, support, protect and celebrate the creators of music. Our Ivor Novello Awards are the ultimate accolade for songwriting and composing in the industry. The Ivors celebrate the talents of songwriters and screen composers, whilst The Ivors Composer Awards celebrate music creators in the classical, jazz and sonic art fields. The Ivors Academy Gold Badge recognises those who have supported and inspired our community. Each of these celebrations has at its heart the power and brilliance of music creators. We campaign together nationally and internationally to ensure that the rights of our members are protected, especially as our industry continues to rapidly change. And we cultivate talent, creating a community across the country in which our members share their valuable knowledge, creators cross-pollinate and collaborate, and all voices are heard. We guide new talent entering the industry and continue to encourage our members as they progress to greater successes. We are the champions of music creators. We are The Ivors Academy. ivorsacademy.com

Title: UK Licensing Coordinator (UK)
Description: Concord is looking to employ a full time UK Licensing Coordinator reporting to Director of Licensing Operations (UK) in our London office working across our publishing and recorded music catalogues. Company and Department Overview Concord is the independent, worldwide leader in the development, management and acquisition of sound recordings, music publishing and theatrical performance rights. Concord has offices in Los Angeles, New York, Nashville, London, Berlin and Miami.Concord Music Publishing represents more than 390,000 copyrighted works spanning all musical genres and including many of the world’s most celebrated compositions by its most legendary creators. Concord Music Publishing is also home to a diverse group of contemporary songwriters and composers creating important and commercially-successful new songs and musical works. Boosey & Hawkes, the world’s leading classical music publisher, and The Rodgers & Hammerstein Organization are also business units of Concord Music Publishing.Concord Recorded MusicConcord Recorded Music is comprised of five active labels across many musical genres: Concord Records, Fantasy Records, Fearless Records, Loma Vista Recordings and Rounder Records. Concord is also home to the #1 kids’ music brand, KIDZ BOP.The company’s historical labels are managed by its Craft Recordings team, and include such storied imprints as Fania, Independiente, Milestone, Musart, Nitro, Pablo, Prestige, Riverside, Savoy, Specialty, Stax, Telarc, Varèse Sarabande, Vee-Jay and Wind-up. Concord’s master recording portfolio contains more than 12,000 active albums and includes no less than 273 GRAMMY® winners (representing approximately 6.2% of all the GRAMMYs® ever awarded) and over 400 gold, platinum, multi-platinum and diamond RIAA certifications across 215 titles.The UK Licensing team is a vital part of Concord, working alongside the publishing and recorded music sync teams and our global network of sub-publishers to ensure we grasp all synchronization opportunities for writers, artists and various catalogues.The Role Primarily handling licensing requests for TV, film and online uses, the position requires someone with a high degree of organisational skill and a keen eye for details, with an understanding of the intricacies of licensing both published works and master recordings.The candidate must be comfortable dealing with clients, song-writers, artists and their managers on a daily basis, and will be adept at both quoting and issuing paperwork for all manner of syncs. The position will also require someone who is able to efficiently log, track and produce reports on the licensing activity from the UK team and sub-publishers.We seek a highly motivated team player, who is passionate about music and able to perform their duties quickly and efficiently. The post will be full time in a lively, friendly and fast-paced environment based in our central London office.Key Duties and Responsibilities of the UK Licensing CoordinatorDeal with licence requests from clients – quoting on and negotiating fees and terms for projects across all media, and seeking approvals from song-writers/managersManage relationships with key clients – music supervisors, production companies, broadcasters, clearance companiesLiaise with co-publishers and record companies where necessaryHelp inexperienced clients through the process of licensingRespond to copyright queries from clientsProduce paperwork for licencesMonitor return of signed licences and file as necessaryMonitor payment of invoices and chase as necessaryLog, track and report licensing activityLiaise with Director of Licensing Operations, Licensing Manager and Business Affairs department about queries on licencesThis is not an exhaustive list of all activities worked on but is a guide to the individual’s responsibilities.Skills and Attributes2+ years in music industryExcellent communicator, good organisational skills and a keen eye for detailAbility to be personable, tactful and sensitive to requirements of confidentiality and when dealing with customers and clients on the telephone and in written correspondence (letters, emails etc).Self-directed, pro-active and a flexible, willing team member, performing all duties to a high standardKnowledge of Vistex Music Maestro or i-Maestro systems would be particularly relevant, although training will be annumCompetitive pension schemeConcord Bucks – for related gigs and merchandiseAPPLICATION DETAILS Please send a covering letter and CV to angus.fulton@concord.com by 15 November 2019

Company and Department Overview: Concord is the independent, worldwide leader in the development, management and acquisition of sound recordings, music publishing and theatrical performance rights. Concord has offices in Los Angeles, New York, Nashville, London, Berlin and Miami.
Concord Recorded Music: Concord Recorded Music is comprised of five active labels across many musical genres: Concord Records, Fantasy Records, Fearless Records, Loma Vista Recordings and Rounder Records. Concord is also home to the #1 kids’ music brand, KIDZ BOP.
The Role: Primarily handling licensing requests for TV, film and online uses, the position requires someone with a high degree of organisational skill and a keen eye for details, with an understanding of the intricacies of licensing both published works and master recordings.
Key Duties and Responsibilities of the U: Deal with licence requests from clients – quoting on and negotiating fees and terms for projects across all media, and seeking approvals from song-writers/managers

Ability to be personable, tactful and sensitive to requirements of confidentiality and when dealing with customers and clients on the telephone and in written correspondence (letters, emails etc).
Self-directed, pro-active and a flexible, willing team member, performing all duties to a high standard
